lines.x: Laios... Run!

lines.st: The moment she was swallowed whole by the Red Dragon, my sister Falin teleported the rest of us out of the dungeon with her magic.
Our belongings and travel funds were all left behind. However, we had no choice but to venture into the dungeon again to rescue Falin before she's digested.
On our way, we tried eating the monsters we killed to feed ourselves.
It's been tough, but thanks to that, we met new friends, solved all kinds of problems, and learned many new things about the dungeon.
But just as we were venturing deeper, thinking all was going well, we seem to have found ourselves in a new predicament...

lines.x: Falin!
Oh... just a dream.
You're finally awake, Laios!
How are you feeling? Still dizzy?
Not really... but now that you mention it, I am feeling a little hungry.
Where are we...?
The sky, the sun, the woods... and...
Looks like somewhere outside the dungeon, right? But no, it's not. None of us recognize this place at all. Not me, not Senshi, not even Chilchuck.
And look. I've been getting no response at all... I've tried several times, but the microscopic spirits aren't responding to me.

That's strange...
Senshi? What are you doing?
Been examining the soil here.
Look, the soil's moist, deep in color, and starts to smell when ya rub it in your palms. Which means it's got a lot of organic matter packed in there.
Meaning?
Meaning it's just perfect for planting! Aye, just like the soil on the back of a golem!
Who cares?!
Pipe down, Marcille! You spooked the birds and I almost fell from the tree.
Well, I have good news and bad news. First, we've checked our surroundings, and haven't come across any monsters. This place should be safe.
What about the good news?
That was the good news!
So what's the bad news?
The bad news is this place is huge, and nothing like any environment I'm familiar with.
From a high vantage point, I could tell we're on the edge of a forest.
I've never seen anywhere like this in the first five floors, and haven't heard about anything like it, either.
Which means...
Which means...?
Did we stumble into a brand new, unknown area of the dungeon?!
Never seen any of those birds before. Strange. An area of the dungeon? Hmm. Looks more like a whole different place to me.
Could this be similar to... being inside one of those paintings? Were we brought to another world?
Does that mean we'll get to see monsters no one's ever seen before?
And, if we're lucky, to taste them too?!
Do you even realize what you're saying?!
Our top priority is saving Falin, not wasting our time in whatever this place is!
And there's one other thing I should mention. I've tried several times now, but I'm only able to use simple magic here.
That... could be an issue.
What's more...
There's more?
Something's off about the forest and this clearing here. As if someone opened up the soil for planting things. I'm quite familiar with methods like that.
You don't care about my magic getting weaker at all, do you?!
Come to think of it, I did spot some strange wooden huts outside the forest. Maybe there are people living there?
Let's head there and ask around, see if we can learn anything.
Don't change the subject!
Strange wooden huts?
Let's go check them out!
Hey!
We need to be more cautious. We know nothing about this place.
Who knows what's inside? We might even encounter hostile demi-humans.

I suggest you let me go first to...
Hey!
The huts aren't big, but they've got plenty of fencing. The stakes're all planted firmly into the ground, too. Nice and steady.
The part above the ground are obviously short. Most folks could climb over 'em, no problem. I guess they go deep, to keep animals inside from digging their way out.
I'm also seeing some black rocks scattered all over. Some kind of marker, maybe?
Hey! Stop running around like that! What if it's dangerous?
(Whispers) Kensuke?
(Kensuke reacts to monsters nearby. Does that mean...)
Huh?
I told you to stop running off already!
—Ahhhh!
I-It moves! Th-There's something squishy under the rock!
Eeeeek!
I-Is it some kind of insect monster that uses rocks as its shell? So the shell is used for both protection and offense?
Hey, don't just stand around and watch! Take care of it already!
Its hard outer shell is more than enough protection for its abdomen. It covers the neck too, which usually has a lot of nerves.
It wouldn't be easy to kill it with just our weapons.
Marcille, use your magic!
Keep it as weak as you can. We don't want to harm the environment here!
Don't worry, I can't cast anything stronger than this right now anyway!
Hmph, they're as weak as walking mushrooms.
So it is a new type of monster! Are you alright, Marcille? How did its attacks feel?
I'm fine. Thanks for worrying about me.
Hm... What a hard shell. You don't often see shells made of minerals.
Wh-What about the rest of it?
(Flips the Originium slug around with his axe)
Wha—?!
What? What is it like?
Stay back!
Don't lay a finger on it when we don't even know the first thing about it.
Critters that crawl on the ground usually live off of decomposing organic matter. So they're likely to be full of bacteria and parasites. You could get infected just by touching it.
That's true...
(Hm? Kensuke? We found the slug already... Is there something else?)
Hold on... The rocks around us have all come to life!
In that case, we'll just have to take them all down!
Haa... Haah... How's this?!
Watch out!
Seems like they come in all kinds! Some can spew some sort of fluid! Careful... It seems to be corrosive!
W-Wow, they can spit corrosive fluids?!
You're excited about that right now?!
I don't think I can do much damage to these guys. They're all yours!
Quick! We'll hide inside this hut!
What...?
Ahhhhhhhh! What the heck?!
Senshi, do you know this orc all decked out in armor?
Hm, all the orcs I know tend to dress pretty lightly. He must be the owner of the hut. Should I ask?
Ask? Ask what? Let's run for it!!
Senshi, put me down already!
I-Is he chasing us?!
What is he, anyway? Is he really an orc?
You sure you don't need me to go and ask?
Why are you people so curious about that? He was obviously hell-bent on slaughtering us with that strange massive blade! We definitely pissed him off!
Yeah, probably with your explosion magic! Why don't you try apologizing?
I'm not apologizing to an orc! And how were we supposed to chase off the rock slugs without magic?!
So this is a new place we've never been to! There are so many things to do here!
For the last time Senshi, I know how to run! Put me down already! Hey—
I've never read anything about this in books either. This is so cool...
Laios!!
Ahhhhhhhh!!!
I still think something about this doesn't add up.
Laios! Is this really the time for that? Who knows when that orc will catch up to us?!
Don't you have even a little sense of danger? This is uncharted territory! Nothing we know about the dungeon is going to help us here!
I didn't expect the slugs to have totally different habits and levels of aggressiveness just for having different shells.
But why were they working with that creature as strong as an orc? Are the slugs really intelligent enough to be tamed?
Shh—Pipe down. The orc is here.
(Quietly) H-He's coming!
(Quietly) What the—! Why is he attacking the slugs? Are they not on the same side?
(Quietly) Is he attacking everything around him indiscriminately?
Hold on... I have an idea.
What are you up to this time?!
Hi.
What?
I've had enough of this! If we're doing this, let's do it now!
Marcille, wait!
I'm not going to get myself killed here by some orc! Falin is still waiting for me to save her!
Huh?
Huh?
Kill...? Why would I kill you?!
Do I look that scary?
Cut the crap!
Hey, watch out—
And that's the last of them. It's always a pain when they start running around all over the place.
I knew it. You're not a violent brute who attacks indiscriminately... You just wanted to take care of the slugs for us!
...What?
Hahaha, it's your first time here. No wonder.
You see, I own this little ranch here, and I built these huts with my friends. Took us a whole lot of work just to keep the Originium slugs under control.
You can call me Big Bob. You look like a bunch of wandering vagrants. The wastes are a dangerous place. You ought to rest up here.
Excuse me, vagrants?! We're adventurers.
Is there a difference?
Of course there is!
So they're called Originium slugs...
You're kidding me. You've never heard of Originium slugs?
Oh, we haven't introduced ourselves yet, have we? I'm Laios. This is Senshi, the newest member of the party. And this is Marcille, a mage, and this is Chilchuck the locksmith.
Now that I take a look, you're all dressed kinda funny. Are you actors from Wrankwood?
What's that?
Never mind... Bet you wouldn't get it even if I explained.
Anyway, most folks would know to stay away when they see so many slugs in one place. So why'd you come anyway? They thought you were intruders.
So you raise these slugs here! That's incredible.
Heh, this is nothing. There are plenty of those little guys here. Rather than making enemies of 'em out in the wastes, I figured I'd try putting them to use.
They always get more active this time of year. That's why I came out to the hut. Didn't expect to find you folks waking all of them up and leading them away though.
Seems like I'll have to knock 'em all out one by one and drag 'em back in.
Uh, sorry about that...
Haha, it's okay!
So anyway... you mean they're edible?!
Huh?! Are you crazy?! Do I have to remind you that they were trying to kill us just now?!
And this isn't the time to learn about local cuisines! We need to—
We do eat Originium slugs, though.
Really?
They don't exactly seem domesticated...
Do they have some kind of unique flavor then?
Not quite. Rather than flavor, it's more like... eh, you should try 'em out yourselves! I'll whip up something for you.
I can help.
Huh?
What???
Eat what? These slugs? No! No, no! Hell no!
I've cleaned the pot, Bob. I'd love to see how you cook the slugs.
Great, thanks! First things first: the slugs we just caught can't be eaten right away. Gotta flip 'em over and keep them in water for five days to starve them. Keeps their digestive tracts clean.
What? You're saying we need to wait five days for this?
Ha, you'd starve first. Luckily I already prepared some earlier. Make sure you wear gloves. We'll boil them in water. That'll shrink the meat and make 'em easier to shell.
Why can't you just pry it off as is?
Because this way we can clean out all the mucus inside.
Just blanch them in the boiling water briefly and take them out. After that, we'll take the shell off and give the meat a rinse. See the mucus?
Ugh... Just like slimes...
What's that?
What? You haven't heard of slimes?
Slimes! Like, the super common monster on the upper levels of the dungeon.
So once you remove the meat, you keep heating the shells in the water.
Hey! Don't just ignore me!
As for the meat, there's still lots of mucus right now. We'll squeeze some lemons on it, then rub it hard with salt and flour to get all the mucus off.
Remove all the organs... Take off this membrane... And rinse it a total of five times.
So we can't eat the organs or the membrane? I mean, slimes taste so good when eaten whole.
Feel free to give it a try, if you don't feel like waking up to see the sun tomorrow.
Now you finally put down the slug guts, huh?
Such a delicate cleaning process... Even I am not familiar with many ingredients that need to be prepared like this.
They're Originium slugs. That makes 'em far more dangerous than most animals out there. There's no skipping this step.
The ones I breed on my ranch may be raised in a controlled environment, but even they need to have their innards cleaned.
If you run into any wild ones, don't even try to cook 'em. You could wash those slugs dozens of times and they still wouldn't be safe to eat.
Looks like a huge pain. Can we pick up the pace, please?
No. Ingredients like these need to be handled carefully.
After this step, we'll add some aromatic herbs to the water, like those thorny branches and leaves over there, and boil the slug in it again.
More boiling?
What's the rush, long ears? That's how you get rid of the earthy scent and tenderize the meat.
If you don't mind the smell, though, we can skip the aromatics.
Er, please continue.
Let's add as many chilis as we can. It'll be so spicy you'll feel like you can breathe fire. And then, more firewood. Keep those flames high!

After that, we're pretty much done. We slice the meat, fry up a few more side ingredients, mix 'em up and start stir-frying. As soon as the meat starts to curl, it's done. Any longer, and it'll be too dry.
Remember, as soon as the meat starts to curl.
Whoops. Skipped a step. We should've drizzled some Originium slug beer onto the meat...
No need for that! This is more than perfect as it is!
Let's just dig in already! Then we can get back on the road! No need to waste more time on any extra steps!
...If you say so. Not my problem.
Well then, the Stir-fried Chili Originium Slug is done!
Hey, Big Beard, what're you doing?
Let me try something.
These shells have been cleaned, right?
Yeah.
What is he up to?
Who knows?

We'll put the cleaned slug meat back into the shells, mince some garlic and add a little butter, salt, and pepper... And of course don't forget the missing step: drizzle the meat in some Originium slug beer.
Then, add a bit of minced garlic at the shells' openings...
And finally, put the shells back in the fire facing upwards and roast them for a while.
The flames lick the Originium slug shells and give out an intense wheat aroma.
The oil sizzles as the warm scent of the garlic wafts toward the party.
Then, there is a light, joyful sound—
*crackle*
There we go! Now hurry and take them out.
I've got pancakes ready, too! Come on, let's eat!
Delicious!
Put the meat on the bread, and bite into it with the chilis. Then, take a sip of beer. Try it!
Here, try the pancakes! I added some fowlbeast eggs. You'll love it, guaranteed.
Is that colorful stuff on top crushed candies? It's sweet!
Fine, I'll try it too!
What's this?
Originium slug antennae.
Wow! So good! I like the chewy texture... but way too spicy! It's very different from Senshi's cooking, but still good!
Hm... With the beer, the roast slug doesn't have that earthy taste at all, unlike the stir fry.
I knew it. The result is always better when you don't cut corners. Rushing will do you no good.
No matter how minor a step is, we must take it carefully. That's what's so fascinating about cooking. Marcille, how anxious you were made me realize this.
Hey, I wasn't!
I'm not the kind of person who throws caution to the wind when I'm anxious! I'm only so worried because none of you seem to be!
It's like none of you even want to get back as soon as possible...
This beer is awesome! It's got a different malty taste to it, unlike anything I've ever had!
Well, that's good to hear. I was planning to bottle and sell it, so I brewed a couple batches. Sounds like I'll be raking in the dough.
Originium slug beer? Is it brewed with the slugs? How does that work?
There are many ways. If you raise them in clean wheat husks from the moment they're born, the husks will stick to them, and eventually they'll form shells that keep the smell.
And if you mix those shells into your brew's hops, this beer is what you'll get.
A few other places do it differently. Say, feeding the slugs hops and using their mucus in the brew.
I'm sure it'd taste different, but I like my brews the best!
The same goes for the pancakes you just had. Give those shiny little fellas some fruit, and their shells will end up sweet.
Mmh... *burp*.
I guess I can relax now that I know there's a market for my beer.
All of this is so good...
Anyway, I think Marcille's been so anxious because... we kind of stumbled our way into this place by accident.
To be frank, most of what you guys have said has gone right over my head.
*sigh*... Never mind. I guess we'll have to look elsewhere for answers.
Well, everyone gets homesick. I know that much at least.
But I've never met anyone who knows as little as you guys. It's not safe, traveling like that... Take these with you.
Big Bob quickly packs up some prepared slug meat and beer and hands them over to Laios, along with a book.
Laios flips through the book and finds it filled with crooked, illegible letters.
What's this?
Heh, that's my 'Bob's Gourmet Guide.' I wrote down everything I've seen and whether it's edible or not.
Take it. You seem surprised by even the littlest things, so just follow what it says in the book. Don't starve or get yourselves food poisoning.
...Seems like this is right up Laios's alley.
I can picture the expression on his face without even looking.
Oh, right, I also know of this place that has... uh, a lot of eccentric people. I'm sure they'd be able to help you get home.
I mean, they even got that red-eyed woman on a leash. If anyone can handle this, it's them.
Really? What's that place called?
Rhodes Island.
They've been opening branch offices in even the most remote villages. You should be able to find them pretty easily.
Okay. Rhodes Island. Got it.
(Quietly) ...What a strange name for an island.
(Quietly) Does that mean we'll need to board a ship...?
If you do manage to find it, remember to say hello to Mudrock for me.
Not a problem.
Alright then. I hope you'll find them soon!
Thanks, big guy.
What an interesting bunch.
I sure hope they'll find that... dungeon soon.
Ahem, now back to work!
Hm? What happened to the rest of the food?
Did they finish all of it...? Hm...
*burp*
Cooked Originium slug... yummy!
Hurry up, Laios.
Look at this mushroom! Even the dungeon's walking mushrooms don't grow this big!
Wouldn't it be amazing to see a walking mushroom this size...? Thoughts on having it for lunch later?
Careful now. The Gourmet Guide says jungle mushrooms are basically all poisonous to some extent.
For example, they might give you full body rashes, thicken your blood, or make you throw up nonstop until every organ is down on the floor...
...I'd rather not think about what the guide's author must have been through to learn all that in such detail.
The orcs only taught me how to identify dungeon mushrooms, so we'd best leave these alone.
But there are some animals that eat them!
Still no. If only one or two critters consider it edible, it means it's probably toxic to everything else.
In other words, if we see lots of animals eating this, then it's likely to be edible for us, too.
Right...
(Low roar)
What was that?
(Piercing buzzing)
There's something flying towards us! Careful, everyone!
WAAAAAAHHHH!
(Knocks here and there)
Ha! Now that was an impressive explosion!
A good thing the test site was empty.
Let me see...
Chamber pressure, off the charts. Same goes for temperature readings, and load capacity too!
Seems like it'll need reinforcement to perform well.
Next, final adjustments to the previous configuration, loosen the belt, tighten the screws, and... done!
My newest masterpiece, the 'Invincible Cool Thing' will be fully operational. As long as I don't run into another accident!
Oh, I should get rid of all these explosives first.
Safety first! Avoid open flames...
(Knocks here and there)
*cough* *cough* That thing over there keeps blowing up...!
*cough* *cough*...!
You alright, Marcille?
I-I'm fine! Just a little dizzy...
(Low roar)
There's more...! Careful!
A golem? Maybe a gargoyle?!
Taste my magic—
BOOM—!!!
High Priest, High Priest? You there?

...What was that?
—Wagh!
A crocodile?! Or a beast-man with a crocodile head?!
Huh? What's a beast-man?
He can speak too!
Why wouldn't I be able to? Hey, you there, Durins, did you crawl up from the underground?
'Durins'?
Is he talking about us?
See any other short ones around here?
Are you saying me and Chilchuck look like the same race to you?
Don't you guys have 'Half-foot' here?
...What are you talking about? Is something wrong with you all?
Right, have you seen our High Priest here?
High Priest?
Old guy, shorter than me, wears glasses.
What's he doing in such a dangerous place?
Dunno. He thinks in mysterious ways.
Well, we haven't run into any old men.
He also moves in mysterious ways.
Guess I should just give up and get back to the tribe, then. Like all the times I went out and looked for him before.
Goodbye, strange tourists!
Wait! Could I ask if you've heard of Rhodes Island?
Heh. Lucky for you, but also unlucky. Because I feel like I have, but also not.
There's someone else in the tribe who I'm sure knows where this Whatever Island is though.
But that person happens to be...
That High Priest you're looking for?
Bingo.
How's this? Come back with me. We hardly ever find him when we go looking, but he always comes back sooner or later.
So basically, you guys were on an island with a huge dungeon, and somehow ended up here?
More or less. Do you know anything about it?
Uh... What's an island?
Huh? It's... a landmass completely surrounded by sea.
What's a sea?
Um... a large body of water?
You mean a lake? You really like to use strange words.
Well, is it because we're inland right now and you haven't heard of...
It's because your questions are so strange! I've never heard of a sea, an island, or a Merini Village. Were you swept here from the other side of Terra by someone's tail or something?
'Terra'?
It's what we're standing on right now.
Marcille tries to read the crocodile man's face for any sign he was pranking her.
Unfortunately, the only message she gets from his eyes is 'Am I talking to a fool?'
So in short, the one who calls the shots in this tribe of crocodiles—er, Archosauria—is the High Priest. The one Marcille blew up earlier was his...
His mech.
The 'Invincible Cool Thing.'
But if we blew up his 'mech'... would he still be willing to help us find Rhodes Island?
It's fine. It would've exploded with or without you.
Always manages to blow up somehow.
The High Priest is a friendly man. I'm sure he won't blame you if you just explain everything.
He's also a righteous man. He wouldn't leave a group of lost visitors stranded.
That's our High Priest!
Still, we should come up with a way to make amends.
Don't worry about it—wait, what's that good smell? Who's cooking today?
The Durin with the huge beard.
Terra, Acahualla, living paintings, the dungeon...
Ugh... I can't wrap my head around it all.
I can use magic here, so at the very least some of the laws of reality are shared...
Falin... I bet I'd be as excited as Laios if you were here.
*sigh* I miss our days back at the academy.
Back then...
Wait, the berries that those little birds are eating...
They look like that fruit Falin offered me back then...
Smells great. Are Durins good at cooking too?
For the last time, I'm not a Durin... *sigh* Never mind, suit yourself.
Quick, round everyone up to try out this bearded Durin's cooking!
Right, you guys were looking for the High Priest?
Yeah. We want to ask him some questions.
It's true he knows much, but it's hard to meet him. It's easy if he wants to see you; but impossible if he doesn't.
Sounds like your classic sage type... So how do we get him to want to see us?
Hmm... Zumama said that if we wanted the High Priest to share his wisdom, we should make some noise to attract him!
Noise... would a big feast count?
Yeah. Something like that.
No objections here. Let's ask Marcille what she thinks.
Speaking of which, where is she?
She hasn't returned yet?
Damn you, Red Dragon! GIVE FALIN BACK TO ME!!!
My fin-drying rack! My bed!
Laios!
Since when was Marcille this quick?!
I think she's seeing us as the Red Dragon now!
Then what do we do?
Seeing how she only has Falin on her mind now, I should try...
Marcille! It's me, Laios! Falin's brother!
No way! You're way too normal! Laios is the guy who'd intimidate a Basilisk with just a strange pose!
I mean, she's right.
Fine, if that's how it's gonna be!
RAAAH—!
Laios... it really is you?
That actually worked?!
Laios! The Red Dragon ate Falin, we need to defeat it so we can revive her!
Wait, why are you pointing at me?! What part of me looks like a dragon?!
Aagh!
What's her problem?
Ghah—Are her eyes rolling about as she stumbles everywhere?
Yes!
Is she lashing out and babbling nonsense?
Yes, yes!
Foaming at the mouth?
Yes, yes, yes!
Then she's poisoned! Lucky for you, but also unlucky, the only one who knows how to make the antidote is the High Priest!
All we have are Kbibi grass that we use as a general cure-all for poisonings, illness, or upset stomachs. You can try using them, but I can't guarantee it'll work.
Then... I'll hold Marcille down, while you guys go look for something to attract the High Priest!
How? Where?
A sumptuous feast ought to draw him in! She must be hallucinating, and if we don't hurry, she's going to blow my house up!
Chilchuck, go with the others! I'll handle it here!
Got it... we'll leave her to you!
Phew, that should be enough fruit and fish. Thanks for guiding me, friend. But could I trouble you with one more thing?
Fish? This is a fin.
Oh... so that's what you call them here. Anyhow, I'd like you to return to the village first and boil me a pot of water.
Water?
Aye, I just thought of something. A regular meal probably wouldn't be impressive enough, so I plan to make a huge stew!
On one hand, consider it a thank you for everything you've done for us, and on the other, didn't you say the High Priest likes lively feasts?
Oh! You want to make a nice pot of stew? Great! I'll head back and get it started!
Phew...
I have enough fruit and fish, but...
I still feel like something's missing...
Odd. Heard a strange noise and came over, but there's nothing here.
And my 'Invincible Cool Thing' exploded too. My luck has been terrible today!
Fine, fine. I'll just go home and sleep it off!
*Sigh* I wouldn't be out here all on my own if I hadn't promised Zumama I'd look after the research projects.
Hmm?
Plump and vibrant colors... A healthy bird. Let's check Bob's Gourmet Guide to see if its edible...
'Fowlbeasts are creatures covered with feathers that can fly. Edible, and delicious no matter how you cook them.'
Hmm... this should make for the perfect main ingredient. Eye-catching too. I'm sure the High Priest will love it! This is it!
(Why is there a Durin approaching me with a hungry look in his eyes?)
(Hm? Hold on. Is he really a Durin even?)
(...Ha, I see. How interesting.)
(Wait, it's been so long since I last played dead. How do you do it?)
SQUAAAAAWKKK!!!
...I don't think I hit it that hard?
Grk...guh...
Well, as long as its dead.
Chilchuck! Have ya gathered enough fruit? Let's head back!
Laios, I know you're Laios, but why do I keep thinking you look more like the dragon?
Marcille, let's talk about Falin instead.
Yeah, she's my best friend! And, listen to me, she's so happy that you're her brother, Laios. She really likes you and always talks about you.
Everyone else considered her a terrible student, but I disagree. I learned so many things from her! I... I love her!
But... but... I was too weak, so she... she got eaten by the dragon!
...It's not just your fault, Marcille.
She's my sister, of course we're going to save her. I'll never give up.
However, Laios cannot help but feel a little curious: how exactly does everyone look in the eyes of the poisoned Marcille?
Does Senshi look like a short and stout dragon? Is Chilchuck a tiny one?
Should he go look for some of the same fruit to find out for himself?
How's Marcille?
She just fell asleep... They said it's how the body protects itself from the toxin, but it's still better to get the antidote sooner than later.
Good! I'll go get the stew started then, with this fatty bird I caught as the star of the broth!
I really hope this'll attract the High Priest...
Help me with the prep work and it'll be done sooner!
Check which way the wind is blowing... and build your fire downwind to avoid starting a forest fire.
Wash the fruits, mushrooms, and potatoes, then cut them into bite-sized chunks and put them aside.
Add some spices and Kbibi grass to the pot, and bring it to a boil so the meat will absorb the flavor better.
Then add the fruits and mushrooms for bringing out the umami taste, cook a few minutes more, then add the meat and potatoes.
Scale the fish, then slice open the belly and remove the organs. Wash the gills with running water or simply remove them with a knife.
Make diagonal cuts on the fish. Mix chili, pepper, salt, and lemon juice all together, then smear the mixture on the cuts...
Skewer them on clean branches and place them right by the campfire. Don't forget to rotate them now and then to ensure they're evenly cooked...
Now let's check back on our bird. When the broth starts to thicken, add some salt and have a little taste test!
Finally, simmer on low heat until that mouth-watering smell comes out...
Mmmm, and it's done! A pot of stew for the High Priest!
How do you usually get his attention?
Sing! Dance! Cheer! Feast! Then he'll show up naturally!
Alright, then let's dig in, everyone!
Mm, the fruit and mushrooms enrich the flavor along with the herbs' unique aroma. A good stew.
Ah... it smells so good...
Sing! Dance! The High Priest won't come unless you get moving!
O-Okay...!
You too, Chilchuck and Senshi!
Uh... is the High Priest still not here yet?
Ugh...
We'll have to use our ultimate method then. An earnest plea!
High Priest! Delicious food awaits you, so please come!
...High Priest! Delicious food awaits you, so please come!
Uggh...
Gukhh!!
Slurp... Mm... mm...
*Gulp*
So warm...
I haven't had such a pleasant bath in a long time.
But there are too many strange ingredients in here!
Half-cut lemons, sliced mushrooms, and cubed potatoes?
Chili pepper and Kbibi grass too, even!
Slurp... Mm... mm...
Mm... Ugh! It tastes weird!
Is this a medicinal bath?
Oh... it feels like my butt is being broiled... Reminds me of the heat inside the cockpit before the mech takes off... This really is quite pleasant...
I'll just soak it in and all the exhaustion from the explosion will disappear!
Th-The bird came back to life?!
It revived in the stew? Could it be...
...Oh, is it an 'undying monster'?
High Priest! What are you doing inside the pot?!
HIGH PRIEST?! This bird?? He was here the whole time?!
Ew... so that broth was his bath water?!
Ugh! I just drank some!
(Oh... I wonder what the undead taste like...)
Yes, I'm the High Priest!
And during my bath, I figured out what you all want from me!
Seeing how your companion is poisoned, I will certainly not sit idly by!
I don't remember mentioning that...
Ahem! To cure your companion...
The best method is to drink this medicinal bath water!
I bet even a crazy feral wolf would come to its senses after having such a disgusting stew!
D-Disgusting?
If it's bad, I'm sure it's the main ingredient's fault.
Since the High Priest has spoken, please have your companion try the blessed stew!
Ugh... *Gulp*...
What a strange taste...
...Urgh... it's so noisy...
Great! The High Priest Stew really worked!
High Priest... Stew?
Oh no!
Marcille struggles to open her eyes—
A monster that can speak! And still alive! The monster is currently sipping the soup in the pot, which shares the same aroma as the bowl of stew before her.
At the same time, Laios already has spoon in hand, and goes in for a quick scoop, sending it straight to his mouth.
Marcille licks her lips, finally comes back to her senses, and lets out a shriek—
M-MONSTER?!
AHHHHH—
I DON'T WANT IT—
I'M SICK OF EATING MONSTERS!
Heh, the stew's pretty good.
Hahaha! You need your tastebuds checked, my boy!
High Priest...?
Shh! I know what you want to know! I already have the gist of what's happened... Hmm, I see, but it's not the time yet.
The time? For what?
Oh, nothing... You're looking for Rhodes Island, yes?
Yes, Rhodes Island can help you!
You're moving in the right direction! Just carry on as you have!
Huh? He's gone? Again?
He said we were heading in the right direction? Sounded to me like a whole bunch of nonsense...
...Because we're not even heading in any particular direction!
So, Marcille... why did you eat the fruit?
...They looked like raspberries.
Raspberries?
When I first met Falin, she offered me some. So when I found fruit here that looked exactly the same...
I thought they were raspberries too, and there was a little bird eating them, so I took two...
Well, you can tell her what happened later.
Yeah, and about all the trouble you caused while you had food poisoning!
Ugh, enough of that!
Let's continue our search for Rhodes Island!
*huff*... *huff*...
(Humming)

I don't see anyone out here in the wilds. How much longer are we gonna have to walk?
Hey, you're being way too chipper about all this.
(Humming) That's because I managed to get one of this world's magical items!
Sure, but it's just some trash no one else wanted.
Hmph, don't tell me you wouldn't be happy-as-can-be if you found a new type of lockpick?
Ever since coming to this world, I've found it difficult to use my own magic. Instead, there is a unknown form of magic here that I can't use.
But now, I have this D32 Steel flashlight!
The factory owner said this 'D32 Steel' can withstand incredible amounts of energy, and that local mages use it to make magical items. Maybe I could use it to channel my magic better!
I even saw him use it to fire off something like a flash spell!
I still believe that nothing in this world comes without a price.
Maybe he didn't want to let a perfectly good tool go to waste, so he gave it to an amazing mage like me, heh heh.
I'm so hyped up about the principles behind this world's magic now.
Well, why not get in some practice? How about using it to get the fruit down from that tree over there?
It's rare to see fruit trees here, and there's a few by the water. We might not get another chance.

You just want to taste the fruit...
But fine. Just for practice. I'll give it a go.
Hmm...
Hyah...!
A moment of silence.
Nothing's happening.
B-But how?! The factory man could use it just fine, and I can feel it responding to me!
Maybe you're imagining things.
Hmph! I'll give it another go!
Grrrrrrhhhhhhhh...!
Yet another moment of silence.
Still nothing.
Can you really get this 'flashlight' thing to work, Marcille?
Why not go back to using your staff?
I just need to get used to it! Give me a moment and I'll understand how it works.
*Deep breath*
Hyah!
Oh! You did it!
Marcille, I knew you could—eh?
Why'd you blow yourself up?
*cough* *cough* *cough* *cough*!
*cough* *cough* *cough* *cough*! *cough* *cough* *cough* *cough*!
Look, no need to push yourself...
Why... Why does magic come out of the end?!
Why are this world's magic items so hard to use?
This is why I hate magic.
That's different! *cough* *cough* *cough*!
One more time! This time I'll get it right, for sure!
Excuse me! I think this D32 flashlight has some issues—
No way! I gave you my most prized flashlight for free, and now you're back looking for trouble?
Ahem... No, I'm not! I just want to ask if you happen to sell any books that explain how it works? I'd like to study it if I could...
No. What kind of place do you think we are? We're a factory on its last legs. We don't have anything but the weapons and mining machines!
If you don't want it, then give it back! Hmph. I knew it. No one can truly understand the beauty of it. You can even use it to light up the entire night sky!
Nobody appreciates this sort of handy gadget... They don't sell at all... And now my factory's about to go out of business... I thought I finally met someone with good taste...
I-I didn't mean it that way! It's fine, it's fine, I'll just give it another shot once I've done more research on this world's magical principles! Uh, I mean... Thank you for the gift!
We should get back to our search for Rhodes Island.
*sigh* Into that wasteland again? This world's way too big. We'll die of exhaustion before we walk there.
Hmm... I don't think I've seen any carriages, but...
Excuse me, this, uh...
Truck?
Oh, so it's called a 'truck'... How much does one cost?
Hah! My factory might be going bust, but this truck's still worth a pretty penny. I don't think you could afford one.
Oh.
I really doubt we could reach Rhodes Island by walking all the way there...
Here, boss. Lunch.
Boss, we're nearly bankrupt. You should sell whatever we have left, otherwise I'm worried about us getting our wages.
We've been eating carrot rice balls for yonks now! You said we'd have roast golden carrots for our farewell party today, to strive for a better tomorrow!
Look, I know I need money to feed you guys, but if I sell off that truck, I'll have nothing to my name!
Alright, you all, we're about to start prepping for our party, so I'll leave you to your business.
A farewell party?
*sigh* It's not like there's anything to prep. All we have are carrots and rice.
The very first meal we had when this place opened was golden carrots...
*sigh*...
If I had any golden carrots to give you, I'd give them. But right now...
If I could, I'd love to continue the tradition. If I could.
So it's a tradition for you to eat golden carrots here?
Yep.
We'd always have a golden carrot dish for our get-together meals.
But looks like we have to give up on the idea today. Seriously, where are we gonna find any? I suppose barbecue and carrot rice will have to do for the party.
Mind if I have a look at your ingredients?
Just this basket of carrots and that meat over there.
What're you doing?
I understand the situation here now.
Leave the ingredients to me, will ya?
When I'm done cooking, you'll be able to say your goodbyes with a smile.
You...
(Whispers) What's on his mind?
(Whispers) Did you forget? Senshi joined us because he couldn't stand watching Laios cook.
(Whispers) He doesn't like seeing other people have sloppy meals.
Eating's not just for survival. It's got emotional value, too. I know how you must be feeling right now.
I'll help you get some of those golden carrots.
R-Really?
You've got plenty of regular carrots, and I can certainly do something with those. But what's a golden carrot exactly?
Is it just... a gold-colored carrot?
You lot aren't Rim Billitonians, are you?
Golden carrots are a traditional delicacy around these parts. Ore and carrots, Rim Billiton's two specialties!
You eat ore? How do you cook it?
Eat ore? No no no! The gold dust on golden carrots comes from the secretions of Goldglitter Originium slugs. That kinda thing is just outta our price range.
But if you could find some Goldglitters, we'd be able to make some right now, no problem.
I see.
Where do these slugs live?
In mines, out in the wilds, anywhere there's not a lot of people.
See that pit over there? Definitely got a slug nest in it, and the mine there's got two more. That tunnel further ahead is overgrown with grass, which means it's already abandoned by the slugs.
Most of the local Goldglitters get snatched up by trappers, though. Bet it'd be hard to find one.
Huh...
Well, the factory might be shutting down, but I still haven't finished all my work for the day. If you lot can find some Goldglitters, we'd never forget!
(Whispers) I am a bit hungry, but we should do something about getting to Rhodes Island, right?
If we do manage to get these golden carrots, would you mind selling us your truck at a discount?
I... I'll consider it.
There must be slugs around here. I can hear them crawling around!
Whoa... There's loads of them!
How're we supposed to find a Goldglitter in this crowd?
Only Big Bob can calm them down. We've seen it, their attacks hurt like heck, so I don't think it'll be easy to get their secretions or whatever...
Looks like we've got no choice but to fight.
Understood.
Stand back! I'll try using the magic item again. I'm definitely getting it right this time!
Marcille lifts the D32 flashlight, shuts her eyes tight, and tries her best to sense the energy stored within.
She blew herself up last time. Can she really make it work?
No, stop!
—What is it now?!
Senshi's interruption does nothing to stop the flashlight. In fact, it begins to grow even brighter.
Don't kill them all at once, Marcille! You have to take them out one by one—
I think we've got a bigger problem than that now, Senshi.
Marcille's thingy looks like it's about to—
Marcille begins to glow with a dazzling light, her hair fluttering with power.
Gah! My eyes!
Wha-What's happening?!
I think the slugs noticed us!
I see them... B-But I still can't control this thingy very well...
Calm down, Marcille. Try it again. It might work.
Feel the flow of mana being drawn in from all around. Channel that power through my fingertips and into the tool, then release—
Studying and using magic requires one to surpass their limits of focus, preciseness, and sincerity, over and over again.
But what one truly requires is an understanding, acceptance, and awareness of the philosophy of magic!
Right at this moment, Marcille's curiosity for the magic of this world has allowed her to create a miracle—
It worked?!
It worked... right?
(Cautiously stop)
I'm not too sure. It's like I managed to release something, but it disappeared.
But I can still sense it... Stay on your toes!
It's going to be a lot more violent than I expected!
IT'S ABOUT TO EXPLODE!
They run behind a rock, huddling tight as they cower behind it, praying that it will hold against the blast.
After an eternity of waiting, a foreboding sound begins to rise from beneath the ground.
The earth creaks and groans.
What kind of sound was that...?
(Carefully back away)
I don't think any of those are Goldglitter slugs, though.
How about we get out of here first and look someplace else?
I feel like the soil's loosened up a bit.
Yeah, I also heard something break beneath us. Could it be Marcille's magic that went below our fee—
—eeetAHHHHHHHHH—
Careful! I think we fell into a mine!
Like the worker said earlier, the slugs made a nest here!
They colonized this place because the factory's about to shut down?
Anyone see any Goldglitter slugs?
I think these are just normal slugs.
Let's go before they notice us!
...Don't see any Goldglitters here either. They're all the normal kind.

'Bob's Gourmet Guide' says that Goldglitter Originium slugs are a common variety in the wilds. 'They live within cracks in rocks or in caves,' and 'they behave identically to other varieties of Originium slug'...
So we should be able to find some here...
...Do you really not feel anything on top of your head?
Huh? Ah!
This is an Originium slug larva! Looks like its shell has just grown in, so it's still rather soft.
And it's busy secreting mucus to harden the shell, which raises its body temperature. It's actually quite snug and cozy having it crawl on my head like this.
Want to try, Marcille?
Absolutely not!
I have a bad feeling about going any further.
It sounds like there's other animals in here.
We should head back.
We still haven't found any Goldglitters, though.
Do they really live here?
Maybe we can use something like golden powder instead for the carrots?
Let's go a little deeper. I don't want to let those lads down when they're hoping with all their heart.
And if we don't find those slugs, we might not be able to buy that truck.
True...
Shh, walk quieter!
There's a group behind us, don't think they're Goldglitters though... They don't look gold, at least.
What are they?
Acid Originium slugs!
Remember? Big Bob said their mucus is extremely corrosive! Don't get splashed by it!
Marcille!
Got it!
Don't overdo it and kill them all!
That really doesn't matter right now—
(Incants)—
Ah!
Summoning magic? No, it takes too long! Defensive magic? Don't think I have enough mana...
Crap, I still can't use my staff properly here.
Oh, right. Flash magic?
I'm going to try the flashlight again!
If the factory head was able to make it shine, then I should be able to use it to amplify my flash magic... right? But it might not be that useful against a group of monsters...
Whatever you do, do it quick! There's more of them by the second!
Fine! Here goes... Flash!
Whoa! You took them all out at once!
Whoa! I did it! I think I'm starting to understand how to use the local magic!
You're the best, Flashlight! You're the first magic item I managed to use here!
Now's not the time! Their mucus got splashed everywhere!
Be careful! Make sure it doesn't touch your skin!
Wait, the flashlight, it's...?
What's happening?
The light coming from the tip of the D32 flashlight begins to dim, its components sparking and sizzling.
Before Marcille can even respond, thick smoke pours out of it as she drops it on the ground.
Flashlight!
Marcille! Don't zone out now! You got splashed by the mucus too!
*sobs*... My flashlight...
I think it's broken.
Why don't we see if the factory owner can fix it once we get back?
*sobs*...
Hmm?
This mucus...
It smells pretty good, quite unlike the mucus from last time. It's more like...
It's... sweet and sour!
Really?
You're right!
Ergh... You guys really need to stop putting bug mucus in your mouth.
Hmm.. Tangy. I like it!
And what a beautiful color too! It's all bright and gold!
Gold, you say...?
The magic shot out from the flashlight turned the mucus gold and tasty...
I think we can use this as a replacement for the Goldglitter slugs!
Hmm... The once-corrosive mucus became edible under the influence of magic... How fascinating.
But this type of mucus is normally stored inside the slug's body. We should gather it all up and make it edible first!
Don't tell me you want to use these on the ground...
No, ingredients must be clean. These are fine for a taste test, but absolutely not for cooking.
And there's not enough of them to make a dish anyway.
If we're gonna make golden carrots, we'll still have to gather the mucus inside the slugs.
And how do we do that?
The ones from earlier were scared off by Marcille's magic. Before they return, we gather it from the ones that're still stunned.
I'm pretty sure the flashlight is broken, though.
Why not try using your staff?
Alright... I'll try...
Hmm...
Did it work?
Looks like... no, the mucus hasn't changed.
Maybe give it another go?
Hrgh!
How about now?
Still no change... But why? Your magic seems to be a bit on-and-off as well, Marcille.
Yeah, it's been like this since we arrived here. The only time I was able to gather enough mana was when I managed to use the flashlight successfully.
The factory head did say that D32 steel can withstand incredible amounts of energy.
The flashlight might have some amplification effect? The flash magic by itself wouldn't be enough in either the mana-gathering or casting stages.
Which means, I still need...
Hey, Laios, stop playing around with that slug! Are you even listening to Marcille at all?
Times like these, we just need to do what she says. This is her field of expertise, after all.
I've got it!
Got what?
With just my staff and the mana I have now, I can never change the mucus into what we need.
We'll need a conductor like the D32 steel to amplify it! There's no point doing any more tests with my staff!
We still need that flashlight!
I think if you tried using it again while it's smoking like that, we'd just end up stunned like the slugs.
Hey! When have I ever messed things up that badly?
The only reason you guys are making progress in this discussion is because I used the flashlight to make the mucus edible!
*Sigh* One moment, I managed to get a bit of an understanding of the local magic, and the next, my flashlight...
It doesn't seem to have melted at all from the acid. That 'D32 steel' stuff must be this silvery metal, right?
Well, Marcille just said the material is what's important, so it should be able to discharge magic no matter the shape, right?
What do you mean? Well, I'd assume so...
But why are you asking that all of a sudden?
Hmm?
Hmm?
...Hmm?
Why are you looking at me like that?
Nooooo! Flashlight! My flashlight!!! Stop! Don't make it into a pot!
The sooner we make these carrots, the sooner we find Rhodes Island, and the sooner we can save Falin, okay?
Hup!
Hah!
Look, it's not that bad. Remember that time my lockpicks were used to dig mimic meat out of its shell?
But Flashlight is different!
That's what I said about my lockpicks.
Pot's finished!
No! NOOOO!!
It's just in a new form, is all. As a tool, it's still as handy as ever.
The form's really important, though!
So why don't you try doing that to the mucus now?
Rest in peace, Flashlight...
We shared such happy times together...
Don't worry. I won't let your sacrifice be in vain.
(Incants)—
Just a few things to go. Get these all out of here, and sell them to the scrapyard.
Man... I really don't want to do this...
We used to cook and eat here... Midnight snacks, barbecues, parties...
*sigh*...
Hmm? Something smells... really good.
Huh... I smell it too...
Where's it coming from? Is someone cooking outside?
I'm cooking up some golden carrots for you folks.
Some... Some what?!
You actually managed to find some Goldglitters?
Nah, but we did find something else that'll turn the carrots golden.
Might not be traditional, but the color will do.
Should live up to your expectations for your last meal together.
By the way, if you hadn't given me that flashlight, we wouldn't have been able to make this dish!
I wouldn't have been able to use my magic without it.
It's certainly not something nobody would appreciate!
R-Really?
Well?
This taste...
I didn't think it'd be sweet-and-sour!
Amazing, right?
It's delicious, though! It has this refreshing and fruity aroma!
Amazing, right?
Hmm... I bet even Big Bob never realized you could prepare acid slugs like this.
Just think about it: sodas, smoothies... There're tons of good things you can make from them!
Thanks, boss. I mean it. I never thought we'd be able to eat golden carrot on our last day here.
What're you thanking me for? It's them who did all the work.
I will say though, this doesn't taste a lick like our first meal as a crew, but...
Back then, when we were eating golden carrot together, you said you wanted to join me and make this Rim Billiton's number one factory!
Haha! Who doesn't have one or two wild dreams when they're young?
It's not too late, either. Just need to start from scratch, is all.
Right. Now that there's someone who approves of my product, maybe my dream can live on.
Yeah, why not take your truck around some other towns a bit, and find somewhere else to make it a reality!
Oh right, the truck...
Hey, you guys wanted to buy my truck, right?
What? Oh, right!
Are you alright with it?
Not that we have much money in the first place...
I'm definitely not selling it off. I still need it for my livelihood.
Oh...
But I can take you anywhere you like, as long as it's in Rim Billiton!
Really?
Really.
And for free, too!
Consider it my thanks for the golden carrots!
C'mon, eat up, then I'll take you where you need to go. As long as the engine's not kaput, we can go anywhere!
*rustle*, *rustle*—
A sudden noise interrupts Chilchuck from his dream.
Ever since he arrived here, even the slightest noise would stir him awake.
He fails to make sense of his dream and stands up, looking towards Laios, who is keeping watch.
Fortunately, the tallman who's usually blunt and awkward is very reliable when it counts.
He has already awakened Senshi and Marcille, and has his weapon ready.
Chilchuck, we'll draw their attention while you scout them out.
Got it.
Chilchuck discovers the sound came from a pack of monsters they've seen before, and they had yet to notice them.
He breathes a sigh of relief and returns to his companions with the news.
Since arriving in this world called Terra, the adventure party has seen too many strange sights.
They exchange glances, and make sure everyone is ready for combat.
Alright, let's go!

As he charges, Laios can't help but think of all the dangers they'll need to avoid in this unknown world. Whether with the help of that Rhodes Island or relying on themselves, they'll have to return home.
Phew... it's finally over.
I was having such a wonderful dream too. What a waste.
Considering how carelessly you spread out when you sleep, you must have wonderful dreams every day.
I don't... Wait, I don't do that, do I?
No, I was just making an educated guess.
You know, we've been walking through this forest for a few days now.
Are there really Sami tribes living here?
The Sami locals like to live in odd locations, and most aren't exactly welcoming of outsiders.
So it won't be easy to find them.
True... *sigh* I didn't think they'd live in such a remote place.
Weren't you the one who wanted to look for a snowpriest, Marcille? Are you giving up already?
Well, I didn't know this place would be so scary!
When we had just arrived in Sami, it looked so pleasant...
I heard all kinds of interesting stories, too. There was a small 'Basque Astrology' tent where they were telling fortunes.
I mean, I did later find out she was a fraud controlling the crystal ball directly, but her story about the 'thousand-headed monster of Minoan myth' was still fascinating.
I wonder if we could actually find it in Minos.
I've also heard tales of a powerful monster that gains the abilities of whatever it eats.
I wish I had that ability...
Of course that's the part that gets your blood racing.
Let's see if we can find a snowpriest before my legs completely give up on me...
The strong adapt to their circumstances, Marcille.
Well, don't worry about me adapting to this environment, because I'm never coming back here. Ever.
Thinking like that will only hinder your growth.
I'd much rather trek through a place like this than stroll down a paved street.
Look, I'm not saying we should give up on the snowpriest.
But the fortune teller said we'd find them in the Myrkwood, and what have we found? Nothing!
It's dark, damp, there are just as many monsters as there are trees, and our supplies are nearly gone. And THAT means we're going to be eating monsters, again.
What do you think, Laios?
Hmm... the ones we've run into so far don't seem all that different from the ones we've seen before. I thought we'd get to see some rarer monsters in here.
Forests don't give up their secrets that easily. We just haven't discovered them yet.
Speaking of eating monsters, looks like the two of them are already carving one up. Isn't it great, Marcille? You're in for another treat.
NO WAY ABSOLUTELY NOT NO NO NO!!!
Another one?!
Wha?
Looks like I got here too late.
And you are...?
Laios, could you maybe not greet someone while wiping your face with the hand holding your bloody sword and holding monster meat with the other?
Oh, it's fine.
I've seen much worse. Trust me.
You can call me Oakcup. I'm an apprentice shaman.
A flock of fowlbeasts told me your location, so I hurried over.
Though, I'm sure you could have handled yourselves fine even without me.
Oh, please don't worry. I come with no ill intent. Seeing how well you're doing, I won't bother you any longer—
Wait, did you just call yourself an apprentice shaman?
I did.
We entered this forest hoping to find a Sami snowpriest to divine a way home for us. Or at least... to help us find Rhodes Island.
A way home?
Yeah. For whatever reason, we don't even know how to do that.
As for Rhodes Island, a while back someone told us to look for them for help, but we haven't been able to find them.
There are many in the south who are eager to 'guide' others.
Oh, we've been to one of those. 'Basque Astrology.'
Thanks to them, outsiders often come to the same misconception you have.
We rarely predict the futures or wishes of others. We look into the future to ensure our own survival.
We only request Samivilinn's guidance when absolutely necessary.
We pray for Sami to warn us of any dangers ahead so we can prepare for them, but beyond that we live by the laws of nature.
Laws of nature?
No different from how you've been living.
Hunt or be hunted; Live and eat, or die and be eaten.
Aye, I knew I liked this place.
...I have a question.
Go ahead.
Does your Sami show up in dreams?
Hmm... I haven't encountered it myself, but it's not entirely impossible to receive a revelation that way.
After all, who can say if our dreams are true or false?
Did you have a dream about the future?
...Nope.
Alright, Mister Mysterious.
So you won't be taking us to a snowpriest?
Hm... It's not that I'm unwilling to, but I'd find it quite troublesome.
Though, if you really do approach another tribe's shaman, they might just chase you off.
What would take the least time and effort...?
Hm?
Huh? Did the tree beside us just... drop two pieces of bark?
There are strange runes on them, too... Which I can't read, of course.
Oh? Can I take a look?
Here.
Sure enough, Sami has sent you a guide.
Thank you, Sami. You sure have saved me a lot of trouble.
What are these pieces of bark...?
Those are Foldartals. They represent Sami's will itself.
Shamans have many ways of glimpsing into the future, but sometimes, Sami will also proactively cast us a glance, just like this.
Sounds a bit too convenient to me.
I think so too, sometimes.
But on the other hand, wouldn't you have been even more frustrated if you couldn't find anyone to read these Fordartals for you?
...Why are you so casual about all this? Aren't you an apprentice shaman? Do you people not take your work seriously?
I asked my teacher that once, and he answered by drinking wine out of my horns for the next two days. Never dared to ask again.
So can you tell us what's on them?
Of course.
The first one... 'North'?
You mean we should head north?
You're already northward bound, but Sami still says 'north.'
Perhaps It wants you to go even further north.
Even further? What's up there, Chilchuck?
The map says that once you leave the Myrkwood, you'll be in the center of Sami's forest. Further north of that are the huge snow-covered mountains and the endless icefields.
Don't tell me Sami wants us to go to the icefields?
Samivilinn often only casts a vague image.
But if you were to ask me, I'd say so, yes.
Huh?
The endless icefields? I asked around about them, and that's not the kinda place you return from.
But you just said earlier that you don't know how to get home.
An undiscovered path hidden in a place from which none have returned. Perfect, no?
As for the other piece... 'Hermit.' Haha. Interesting.
And what does that mean?
It can mean many things... but if you're talking about a 'hermit' in the 'north,' then there's really only one thing it could be referring to.
The Cyclopes.
THE WHAT?!
You know of the Cyclopes?
I... heard about them when I was gathering information.
Is that a kind of monster?
Um... no, they're a special kind of Sarkaz.
They use a completely different way to tell the future than we Sami do.
And they just happen to live in seclusion, deep within the mountains to the north.
Put the two Foldartals together, and I think this is what Sami is trying to tell you:
Go northward to ask the Cyclopes for help on how to get home.
I think I'm willing to give it a shot. What about you guys?
If there's a chance, we might as well take it.
...If it really works out, I suppose I'll go along.
Chilchuck?
The three of you already agreed to go, so is there any point in asking me?
Of course there is, you're a member of our party too.
...I'll be honest then.
I'm against it.
Do you not trust Oakcup?
That's not it. I can tell he's not lying.
Then why not?
Prophecies, divination... you guys are way too willing to believe that stuff.
What if someone planned this all out? Dropped two pieces of bark here and is trying to cheat us? Again?
But—
He has a point. But I still think there's no harm in heading north a bit further.
If we run into another shaman, we just have to ask them to read the bark and we'll know if Oakcup was telling the truth or not.
We've been here for a while now, so I wouldn't want to miss even a chance of finding a way home.
...Fine.
Speaking of going north though, why do I feel like we've been wandering around in circles this whole time?
Haven't we already walked past here before?
...I made some markers. I'm sure we've been making progress.
What's wrong, Chilchuck?
Senshi's right. But the terrain here is making me feel uneasy.
Like the land itself has been shifting the whole time...
You mean like the dungeon?
Yeah, something like that. But while the dungeon is always changing on the inside, this place is... the entire forest seems to be changing.
Look, there's a cliff up ahead. I'll go scout the terrain.
What was that?
The four look towards the source of the sound, and see a monster resembling a bull.
—Of course, they know by now to call it a 'hornbeast.'
A creature showing up in the forest should be a common sight, but this hornbeast has still drawn their attention... For one reason—
On its face is a black... black? No, it is a blank, an endless void.
The first thing that comes to mind is the galaxy of stars one can see in the night sky.
A cow with a void as its face. They find this reality hard to believe.
What's even more shocking is what lies dead center in the blank space. A circle.
An uncannily perfect circle.
A circle somehow even darker than the void surrounding it.
Darker than the night, deeper than the void.
But how can this dark circle seem to emit light?
Because it isn't. The circle is really a hole that sucks in all the light near it, to the point that it seems to be glowing.
Darker than the night, brighter than the light...
Well... this is a strange monster.
The void on its face doesn't seem to be part of its usual biology.
Fascinating. I really want to study it.
(Howls)
Sounds like it's angry!
There are wounds on its body, from weapons... Someone's riled it up, and now it thinks we're the enemy!
Eat my axe!
(Pained howl)
Wow does that girl have a ton of weapons...
Damn, the cliff's collapsing! Chilchuck!
Laios immediately sprints towards Chilchuck on the cliff, but is one step too late—
Chilchuck tries to jump backward to catch the outstretched hand, but Laios fails to grab him and can only watch as the Half-foot falls off the cliff.
Gah—
You're awake.
...Who are you?
Call me Arges.
I foresaw an accident in the forest today, so I decided to patrol the area, where I found you unconscious in the snow.
...Have you seen my companions?
I'm sorry.
...Tch, I should go look for them.
Gah—
You're quite injured. You should rest here first.
You don't seem to be from Sami.
...My friends and I were looking for a way home, and a guy called Oakcup told us Sami's will was guiding us to go north.
Oakcup... the oak-child.
You know him?
I've heard that in Sami the name is synonymous with 'wisdom.'
'Wisdom'...? Then... Do you know of the Cyclopes?
...What's wrong?
I have witnessed many ensnared by misfortune. Death itself... is no stranger to me.
Yet, why do I see so many deaths in you?
No sign of Chilchuck?
Yeah...
We should at least be glad for the thick snow and trees here. He should have handled that fall easily.
Mm... but he isn't the greatest at self-defense, so we should still find him as fast as we can.
It'll be terrible if he ran into that hornbeast from earlier.
Aye. But we have another problem—
Sorry.
*sigh*...It's not your fault.
But what are you doing all by yourself in this forest, miss?
I'm hunting...
Do you live here, lass?
Nope, I live on Rhodes Island!
RHODES ISLAND?! That's the place we've been looking for.
Huh? You wanna go to Rhodes Island?
Yeah, we're trying to find our way home, and someone told us we could get help there.
You lost your home? So sad...
So can you take us to Rhodes Island?
Eh... But I don't wanna go back yet...
Why not?
'Cause I'm still hungry!
Is that all?
Then if we feed you till you're satisfied, will you take us there?
Mm, sure!
...I didn't expect to find a lead like this.
Could this be what Sami was guiding us towards all along?
But...
I still haven't found my prey.
We have our own food—Ah.
Guess we lost it all in the chaos earlier.
...How about this?
First, you help us find our companion. Then, we'll help you hunt whatever you want. And finally, you take us to Rhodes Island.
Mm! Okay!
What an innocent lass. What's your name?
I'm Ceobe, but you can call me Kay!
You saw death in me...?
Yes.
Many deaths, in fact. Both in the past and the future, yet here you stand now, alive.
That Oakcup guy said Cyclopes can see the future.
Are you one of them?
Yes.
Then please, tell me my fate.
...Cyclopes can only see doom.
That's fine. Just tell me the fates of me and my companions.
Your companions walk toward the endless icefields...
No, they do not go of their own will. They were... swallowed up, by the dark and the void.
What's that mean?
They were corrupted by demons.
Demons?! No one mentioned any demons!
Demons... are a vagrant existence on this land.
They corrode and envelop all that they touch.
Corrupted creatures lose all sense of self, as if swallowed into the void—

lines.st: On its face is a black... black? No, it is a blank, an endless void.
The first thing that comes to mind is the galaxy of stars one can see in the night sky.
A cow with a void as its face. They find this reality hard to believe.
What's even more shocking is what lies dead center in the blank space. A circle.
A circle somehow even darker than the void surrounding it.
Darker than the night, deeper than the void.
But how can this dark circle seem to emit light?
Because it isn't. The circle is really a hole that sucks in all the light near it, to the point that it seems to be glowing.
Darker than the night, brighter than the light...

lines.x: Those creatures have a circle on their faces...
Just like a single eye on a head.
Have you already seen a corrupted creature?!
Chilchuck ponders for a moment before pressing his hand on his injured arm and exiting the cave.
Can a doom you see be avoided?
All I can say is that I try.
...Then so will I.
So you're saying Rhodes Island is a giant ship?
Yeah! It's huuuge and it goes everywhere!
Oh, so that's why we never found it!
*sniff*
What's wrong?
Smells like blood. My prey's blood. The one-eyed monster is nearby!
What a keen sense of smell.
Hm? Kay, did you just say 'one-eyed monster'?
Yeah. A hornbeast with only one eye on its face. So a 'one-eyed monster,' right?
Eek... do you guys still remember what Oakcup said?
About the Cyclopes.
Could he have guessed wrong?
Maybe Sami didn't mean for us to find an actual Cyclops.
Maybe It just predicted we would find Kay, and knew we would encounter a 'hornbeast' like that?
Either way, since we've run into it again...
We'll just have to deal with it.
Is it dead?
Mm.
Phew... Then I guess we can consider Chilchuck avenged.
Now let's go find him.
I've got a suggestion.
What if we started a fire here, instead of running around aimlessly?
It might attract some wild beasts, but Chilchuck could spot us too.
Uh... But—
Either way, we can't really do much on an empty stomach.
You're right.
Even if he doesn't see it, we should feed ourselves first if we want the energy to keep searching afterward.
So what do we do with this 'one-eyed' hornbeast?
Let's eat it! Together!
Aye, sounds good.
But—
I've heard of traditions where people will kill and eat an animal as a sacrifice to the gods, hoping for divine revelation.
My guess is the will of Sami was hoping for us to do something similar.
Then—
Don't even try to pretend, Laios. You were planning to eat it from the start.
Let's start a fire first.
Hopefully, Chilchuck will see it.
It's done!
Ugh—I'm being serious here, the meat looks bizarre.
Already tasted it. Doesn't seem too different from regular meat.
Fine, I guess. We've already eaten stranger things than this.
Wow, it's actually pretty good!
What are you doing over there, Kay?
Looking to see if there's an even bigger one!
Hoo, guess not. No sight of that Durin who fell earlier either.
Come join us for a meal first. We'll go look for him together afterward.
Okay, coming!
Hey—!
Whoa! You're that Durin!
*pant*—*pant*—
And you're the girl who popped out of nowhere.
Sorry I made you fall.
*pant*... *pant*... It's fine. You guys alright?
Mm, we're all good.
Great.
I knew that doom was avoidable.
They're making steaks and skewers over there. Said they'll keep looking for you after eating.
Let's go together? I bet they'll be so happy to see you.
Tch. Of course they're eating even at a time like this. What was I worried about?
Wait, steak? What kind of steak?
The 'One-eyed monster' kind.
'One-eyed monster'?!
You guys... they can't have...
Oh look, here they come.
Perfect timing. I'm going to give them a piece of my mind—
Hey, you guys can't just—
Chilchuck turns around and is about to begin scolding his friends for leaving him behind for a barbecue.
But his words clog up in his throat.
Time to go home, guys.
We have stayed too long in this world.
Falin must be getting anxious.
Yes.
It's time to harvest the crops on the golem. I still have Red Dragon meat to cook.
Their faces, now blanketed by the void, are no longer the familiar visages he once knew.
Their voices start to break and distort, until Chilchuck isn't even sure how they're speaking.
You guys... ate that corrupted hornbeast?!
Chil■■■■■, what are you still ■■■■■ here? Let's go ■■■■.
Where... are you guys going?
North, the northern ■■■■.
End, door, go home, go ■■■■.
There's a door in the north we can use to go home?
Is this Sami's guidance?
But how do you guys know all this?!
We ■■■■, we come from that ■■■■.
■■ return to that ■■■■.
P■th, h■m■, F■l■n—
Chilchuck feels a mess of emotions welling up.
Panic and resignation wash over him—doom feels inevitable. His own party members are now in dire straits, and he is powerless to help them.
But he also feels a little angry...
Because the reason for their distress... is entirely their own damn fault!
Chilchuck is sure he only blinked once, but the trio suddenly appear near him.
The next second, they are by his side.
From his perspective, they seem to have moved in a flash, as if the holes in their faces swallowed the space between them.
Soon, they disappear into the depths of the forest.
Chilchuck stands still for a moment before breaking into a deep sigh.
He hears the Cyclops approaching behind him, and sees the puzzled look on the girl's face.
He does not wait or hesitate, but begins sprinting towards where his companions vanished.
He is too embarrassed to explain what happened here to the Cyclops and her sorrowful gaze.
They are his companions after all... of course he can't just watch them disappear for good like that.
GUYS! COULD YOU JUST—SLOW DOWN!!!
An angry roar echoes in the skies above the Myrkwood, lingering for a long time.
Wait for me! I wanna join you guys too!
Five, four, three, two, one...
There's the bell! Let's go!
Gkh!
Marcille, on your left!
Hup!
Laios, front! Right!
Hup! Hah!
Senshi, behind you! Left! Forward!
Hooh! Hyah—
Front back front right left left right!
Eyah—
Front back left front right left back!
HIYAH—!
*huff*... *huff*... That's all the ink spirits over here handled...
Much appreciated.
Oh, don't mention it! We're happy to help with any of your troubles, considering the village agreed to take us in for a bit.
I'll be off then. Promised to help plant some veggies out in the fields.
Senshi sure has gotten friendly with the locals.
Is it not the same for you, Chilchuck? Uncle Zhang told me to invite you over for a drink with him after you finished with the ink spirits.
He just doesn't want to admit he can't outdrink me.
May I come over for some tea today, Dawn?
Certainly, I am available if you are.
It's your turn to tell tales of your adventures, and I am quite looking forward to them.
Though... I do recall that when you first arrived, you mentioned that you had to save Laios's sister.
Are you sure you are alright with staying here?
It's true that our goal is to save Falin.
But...
It's not like rushing will help us find a way to leave faster.
And I have to admit, life is good here.
We'll talk it over when the time comes. Right, Laios?
Hmm? Sorry, what did you say?
I can't believe you weren't listening to me again!
*chuckle* Your passion for ink spirits is well-known across the village by now, Laios.
I mean, these 'ink spirit' slimes are just so interesting!
They have fixed forms with distinct heads, bodies, and limbs! Instead of enveloping their prey's heads, they attack like other beasts! They even vocalize!
But the strangest part is how, unlike other monsters, they don't leave behind any kind of corpse, just some black water.
I told the villagers all about their weaknesses yesterday, but they didn't seem very excited.
That's because you're the only person who cares about that sort of stuff.
...Huh, is that so?
But even Chilchuck's learned to exploit those weaknesses. The villagers can't seem to grasp them at all. Every time I see them facing ink spirits, it's like they have no idea what they're doing.
Gkh-gkh!
I'm starting to suspect they can even understand us...
It's odd. Senshi said they're not like slimes at all. Slimes have distinct organs, after all...
Alright, alright, I get it.
Hmm? Did some of them slip through?
Hraaagh—
Hmm? That one looked a bit familiar... It's got a mole on its face. Where have I seen that before?
I'm pretty sure I killed the exact same one a few days ago. Why's it shown up again?
What's going on here? ...Wait, could it have come back from the dead? I'm going to go get a better look!
Remember to be back for dinner!
...*sigh* And he's not listening. As usual.
Sorry he's like that.
Oh, it's fine.
Anyway, do you still remember how long it's been since you arrived at P'o-shan Village?

Of course I...
Hmm?
How long has it been since we arrived?
Wait, wait wait wait... There's way WAY too many of you.
Gkh...
We might not have been here long, but we've been killing plenty of ink spirits every day. Who would've thought there'd still be this many left?
There are so many of them here, yet there's no sign of fighting... Could they actually coexist peacefully?
I don't see any of their young, though.
Now that I think about it, I can't tell their genders, either. They're all adults too, no young or old. No matter how you look at it, it's no ordinary monster cluster.
Hmm? Wait, where'd the familiar-looking one go?
Crap, that one spotted me!
I've got to think of a way to stop it from calling over more! I can't take them all on my own—
Oh no, it's about to call out—
Here, have some fruit!
Gkh...haaa? Munch munch...
Munch munch...
Gkh.
What's that, you want more?
Gkh, gkh.
As long as you don't start shouting.
Here, I was saving them as a snack, but... Look, just eat quietly, and come over when you're done. Then we can go around the back of this wall and...
You're awfully well-behaved. What's going on here?
(Sneakily climbs behind the wall)
(Ready to pounce at apricots on the ground)
(Apricots pounced! Proceeds to gobble up apricots)
It just attacked the village, but now it's docile over a few pieces of fruit?
(Nom nom nom)
(Bites onto Laios's pocket)
Hmm? What're you up to now?
You attack the village, yet you don’t seem any happier when you win, and you flee when you lose. Now you're just snacking on apricots!
You do fight over food, but it makes no sense to attack the village for it—there are way more fruits in the mountains. So why attack so often just for a few fruits?
The villagers haven’t stolen your young or encroached on your territories, so where's all this hostility coming from?
Wait, isn't that the one with the mole from earlier?
Take this!
Laios sends an apricot flying at the familiar-looking ink spirit, striking it right in the head with a 'pok!'
It staggers from the blow, but quickly recovers and raises its stubby arms to cover its head.
But that's when the sweet scent of apricot hits it.
Hruuh!
Wait, are you here to eat apricots too?
Here, catch.
Hruuh! Hruuh!
Munch munch...
Let me have a closer look... Same mole, slightly bigger head than the rest, a slender yet forked tail...
You really are the same ink spirit as last time! I thought you were rather odd, what with your belly so full you could barely walk. Hardly a ferocious marauder!
Hruuuh...
Did you really come back to life?
Do you go through the cycle of life and death over and over?
If so, that's pretty similar to our life in the dungeon.
Gkhuu?
I still don't get it, though. If that's the case, then why attack the village in the first place?
Is it just to eat apricots?
Alright. This is the last of them...
...So I followed Falin into the cave.
Which is where I found out her 'secret.'
She also explained what 'ecology' was while we were there. It was the first time I realized how limited my experiences had been.
That's the day she and I became best friends.
What more can one ask for in life but a companion dear?
From the way you talk about her, this Falin must be very important to you.
You're right. She's the closest friend I have.
Shouldn't you be looking for her, then?
I remember you telling me that she was eaten by a red dragon, but you have some means of reviving her.
Red dragon... Red...
We will save her. And once we do, I'll bring her back to P'o-shan to meet you!
Very well.
There goes the bell again. That's rather frequent for one day.
It better not be Laios's fault!
I'll go have a look!
*sigh* This... is about all I can do.
It seems it's up to you to save your companions, Laios.
What's the matter, Marcille?
It's strange. The bell went off, but the ink spirits didn't attack.
Laios hasn't returned either.
Haha, if you like apricots, then go get 'em!
Hup!
He throws a handful of apricots he picked into the distance and a pack of bestial ink spirits, large and small, chase after them.
Laios rejoices in watching them squabble over the fruit. Almost wanting to take the entire pack home to raise as dogs.
Right, they're just like Nussa, Anowtrid, Boff, Fucci, Dustrag, and Muimui!
Hraaagh—
No need to rush, plenty more to go around!
For the ink spirits that resemble sloths, Laios feels no need to make them fight over the fruits.
He's become familiar with the habits of this species of ink spirits, setting aside a pile of apricots just for them to eat as they please.
Once they eat their fill, they lie down, and the color of their backs begins to change...
As he watches them try to stuff their mouths despite their protruding, plump bellies, he wonders how anyone couldn't be fond of these little fellows.
Aaaaaagh—
No need to worry. Did you think I forgot about you guys?
The tortoise-like ink spirits might be the species Laios is most interested in. At first, he thought they were giant rocks, at least until his butt made contact with its shell.
Their temperament is exactly the same as a tortoise. The most interesting thing about them is that once their appetites are sated, spring water begins to flow out the false mountains on their backs!
Thunder rumbles in the distance.
Upon hearing it, the ink spirits begin to stand, one by one.
Though they discover that they cannot, for one simple reason...
They've stuffed themselves silly!
Aye, looks like it's gonna be a good harvest this year.
And it's all thanks to you, Senshi.
If you weren't here, my harvest would only be about half as much!
All I'm doing is putting my knowledge to good use.
Senshi, stop with the farming!
What's wrong, Marcille?
It's been days since Laios went off to look for ink spirits outside the village, and he hasn't returned!
Laios? Well, the lad's fond of research. Let him be.
The ink spirits haven't been attacking the village lately either...
Surely something's happened to him?!
Oh... Well, we were planning on staying here for long anyhow. No harm in waiting a bit longer.
No, no... We can't stay here long!
We need to go beat the Red Dragon and save Falin! Have you forgotten all that?!
Huh?
Now that you mention it, Marcille, how long have we been in this village?
Hrgh—!
Hungry again?
My oh my, you're greedy little fellows, aren't you?
No need to worry, plenty for everyone today too.
Though we're going to do something a bit different this time around.
Gkh—gkh?
Hugkh?
Hah—hoo...
See the peak of that hill over there?
I put lots of your favorite fruit there.
If you want to eat, you'll have to fight for it! It's one of the laws of nature!
GKH—GKH!
