Well, if everyone understands the rules, I'll start counting. Three, two, one...
Go! Get running!
The ink spirits start charging towards the hilltop on his command. All save for the tortoise-shaped ones, that is.
Aaaaaagh—
Oh, you'd like me to sit? You really want me to ride you?
Haha, you're a smart one. You figured out that instead of fighting the others, you can just butter me up.
Hoo—ahh...
Alright, alright. Here, have a bite.
Amidst the mounts, days pass like the wind.
By now, Laios has figured out it's the thunder that triggers the ink spirits to attack the village.
But external stimulus like that won't change their inherent habits. Laios knows this too well.
So why not lure them as far away from the village as possible with fruits?
That way, if they attack again, they'll have to run all the way from the top of the hill rather than halfway down!
Ahh... *hic*... This is some really good stuff...
What do you say, enough?
No... No way!
Another cup, then.
Chilchuck!
Eh? What are you doin' here, Marcille?
Stop drinking already!
Laios has been gone for who knows how long!
The ink spirits haven't been attacking lately either.
He must have done something!
Then let him do his thing, I'm drinking here.
Figures he'd be like us from just a bit earlier.
Right. He's practically a villager at this point.
Chilchuck, do you remember how long it's been since we arrived here?
Do you remember how we need to rescue Falin?
Do you remember those mimics you really really hated?
...Ghh, gah, my head.
Is your memory coming back?!
Chilchuck, are you alright?
Gkh gkh gkh, gkh? (So, what're your names?)
Gkh gkh!
Wuh!
Aaaaaagh—
Gkh gkh gkh wuh! Laios! (I see now, your names are 'Bitey', 'Angry' and 'Lazy'? I'm Laios!)
Wuu gkh, gkh gkh!
Gkh gkh gkhhh!
Hoo—
Gkh gkh huu gkh wuu gkh gkh! (Of course we're friends!)
Secluded in the mounts, Laios loses track of time.
He no longer cares if the thunder booms or not.
He's now good friends with the ink spirits. They eat together and live together. Every day, they pluck fresh fruits and frolic about in the hillside forest.
He even understands their speech—
You sure the lad's around here?
No... But the ink spirits all came from this direction.
If he's been researching them this whole time, I figured he'd be here.
You said that ever since he came out here, the ink spirits haven't been attacking the village as much.
Maybe he did something to stop them?
But I've got a sneaking suspicion he's run off to play with them god-knows-where...
You might be right... We'd better find him, quick!
Phew...
The longer Laios lives with them, the more he has come to understand these 'ink monsters.'
I should head back and tell everyone about this.
They'll be amazed! After all—
Gkh gkh?
Gkh gkh woo! (Come with me. I'll introduce you guys to my companions.)
Wuu gkh—
Wuu wuu gkh gkh! (Don't worry, they're good people. They won't harm you—)
Wuu—
Gkh—gkh— (Yeah, I'm sure they'll welcome you all!)

Laios, we're here to help—
you...
Huh?
The three rescuers see a most bizarre scene.
Laios, the one they’ve been worried about and eagerly searching for...
is crawling around on all fours like a wild animal, talking to the ink spirits in an incomprehensible language.
It was at that moment that they felt something shatter in their hearts.
Wuu gkh gkh! (Oh, you guys came at just the right time!)
Wha...?
Oh, sorry. I've been using their language for so long, I nearly forgot how to speak to humans.
...Sorry to have bothered you. We'll be on our way.
Hey, don't go, I've got some real important discoveries to tell you!
Oh, no, no need. We'll find our own way out and let you live out your dream here.
I was gonna say I learned something about how to leave.
...You learned what now?
Just watch.
Hey guys, it's lunch time!
Gkh—! (Happily scramble to each and every pile of fruit)
Did... Did you tame them with all this fruit?
Hraaagh—
They're changing color!
That's one of its traits. Bitey changes color when it's full.
It's called Bitey?
Mhm. The one that looks like a small dog is Angry, and the tortoise-shaped one is Lazy.
Did you name them?
No, they told me.
Gkh, gkh—blurgh—
Eurgh, this one's eaten so much it puked! And the vomit's so colorful, it's kinda sickening—
Hmm? Wait, that smell... Is that... alcohol...?
What are you doing, Senshi?! That's ink spirit puke!
Smells like fruit wine. Hmm... A good ferment, if my nose is right.
Tastes pretty good, too.
Don't just eat random things!
Calm down, Marcille. Listen.
Listen for what?
The sound of what's sloshing around in their bellies. This one has wine inside, while this one has jam.
...And?
Keep looking.
Aye, this one's got gushing springs on its back after eating all that fruit. Hmm... This... Oh my, how refreshing. It's fruit juice!
What does any of this have to do with getting out of here?
Wait a moment and you'll see.
Senshi, do you have any containers we can keep the jam and drinks in?
I do. I've been drinking with the villagers a whole lot recently, so I got a few cups and bottles on me.
Don't tell me you're thinking of...
Don't tell me you want to...!!!
Nicely done, though this jam will need some sugar if you want it to be palatable.
As for the juice... It's refreshing, sure, but a bit bland.
But if we blend the juices that go well together...
Add in some sugar, stir it until it's mixed evenly...
Pour into a glass, add fruit slices for decoration, and... It's done!
My improved ink spirit wine, jam, and juice! Give it a try!
Oh, this fruit wine really does have new flavors to it.
Bit too much sugar for one day, though. We should get some exercise to work it off.
I really don't want to speak with people who are willing to eat vomit...
Honey is bee vomit, Marcille.
The vegetables I grow on the golems are also fertilized with excrement from people in the dungeon—
Stop! I don't wanna hear it!
What's up, Laios? You haven't said a word since earlier.
I'll tell you all my plans now.
When I was chasing that ink spirit with the mole, I was surprised to discover that they'd happily slack off from attacking the village if it meant they got to eat fruit instead.
Which means...?
Monsters do attack humans, and sometimes that is due to their aggressive nature...
But most of the time there's a specific reason, like their habitat getting encroached upon, or something happened that enraged them.
But there's no reason why they attacked the village. At first I thought it was out of hunger, but these guys are far too docile and meek when they eat to back it up.
Gkh?
Yeah... That one's even dripping with spit.
Then there's the villagers. It's incredibly strange how they have absolutely no understanding of ink spirit behavior.

We've only just arrived and I've already found out you can tame them with fruit. But the villagers have clashed with them countless times. They should've learned a thing or two about handling them, even if they didn't want to.
But they haven't. It's like they have no need for it.
Maybe they tried before, but didn't learn the proper way?

That'd be even stranger. If they tried and failed, wouldn't it make more sense to just leave this dangerous place? Obviously, they chose to stay, so wouldn't they want to keep trying to fend them off?
They won't leave and they won't find a way to fend them off. So how are they still living here?
And haven't you guys ever wondered why we couldn't find an ink spirit 'nest'?
What's more, they can come back to life endlessly. It's as if... this whole place is fake.
Oh? What an interesting theory.
Dawn? When did you...?
Please, do go on.
Where we come from, legend speaks of a 'lunatic magician,' who used his tremendous magical power to create a dungeon.
All sorts of monsters live within it.
But their existence isn't isolated, but interconnected. They prey on, exploit, and sometimes form symbiosis with one another...
They're all part of one ecosystem.
No matter what kind of man this lunatic magician is, I can tell just how amazing of a feat that was. As for this place...
Someone who could create such a vivid, life-like world must be amazing as well.
But...
(Drooling and smacking its lips)
I wonder... why didn't this person design a more complete ecosystem when creating the ink spirits?
Where do they build their nests? How do they reproduce? How do different variations communicate with one another? And what's their relationship to the people who live here?
I'm so curious about all this, but I don't see how all the pieces fit together. Why did that person make things this way?
And there's one more thing I noticed.
They hid away a basket of hawthorn berries, but never once ate from it.
...Hawthorn?
(Whispers) Hawthorn, is it...
(Whispers) I never noticed.
Sorry, what was that?
Nothing, just thinking aloud is all.
But... I see now.
The connection between all things, which you deem important, serves as a mutual affirmation of one another. I am your mirror, and you mine. This is the 'truth' as you understand it.
Marcille, how come I don't understand a word of what those two are saying?
Uh... Umm... I'd love to agree with you, but I think I can understand them a liiiittle bit...
*burst of laughter* Interesting, very interesting.
You are right. All things adhere to logic. As with all the other myriad forms of life within a painting, so too must ink spirits have a place to come from, and a place to go.
I suppose rootless weeds such as them would be flaws in reality.
There have been a few who were able to discern that they were in no more than a painting, but... It's been quite a while since someone explained their reasoning with such conviction.
Perhaps this can be seen as another way of saying 'the mountains are but mountains, and the water is yet water.'?
After saying that, for some unknown reason, she turns and looks up at the sky.
He has seen the truth beyond the veil of wonder, no?
Huh?
What's happened to this painting?!
I painted a perfectly good landscape on this! Why is it all like this now?
The ugly ink blots, the water stain, and the blotches of red and purple... Did someone sneak into my room?
Could it have been...
No, this time it isn't the work of my 'lovely sister'...
How interesting. Perhaps there is an unruly spirit roaming the wilds with a penchant for artistry.
Whoa! I think the whole mountain shook with that thunder.
Oh dear. It seems you've made her truly angry.
What? Who? Who's angry?
And no wonder, what with all the ink spirits you lured over, and how you got them so drunk they can barely even stand. This is the first time I've seen them like this.
Dawn, what are you talking about?
Look! Up there! Someone's... falling out of the sky?!
Uhh... more like flying... and she looks pissed! Maybe she's the magician who created this place?!
Awawawah! Laios must have made her mad with his blabbering!
What?! But I didn't say anything wrong!
No need to panic. She's not here to harm you, just... consider it a unique way to send you on your way.
The ink spirits are simply her distracting thoughts, and as such, I hope you understand why she didn't feel the need to organize them properly.
Hey! Be gentle with them! They've just eaten, you can't lift them upside-down like that!
Gkh—blurghh—
Quick, take this bottle and fill it up with that jam! Don't let it go to waste!
Get—out—NOW!
Wha-wha-what?
How are you drawing with that sw—
—orrrrrrrrdAHHHH—
—Ow! My butt!
Eeeek!
How did we... Are you guys alright?
*cough* *cough* *cough*...I'm here. Still alive.
I'm alright too.
Hmm... I can smell the sea.
Are we close to the coast?
I don't know, but at the very least, we've gotten out of that place. Probably.
No, wait, I didn't even have time to say goodbye to them!
I guess things really did work out thanks to Laios's love of monsters.
Wait, Marcille, your mouth...
Why's it all black?
You couldn't stop yourself from trying some earlier, could you?!
Mmwuh? ...Wait, it's the same with you guys!
Puh! Pfah! Pfeh! Pfoo! What is this taste?
What did we just eat?!
...Looks like this painting's got to go.
I thought they were an amusing bunch. Not going to try some? There are some hawthorn berries here too.
Not even a bite?
Absolutely not!
In their swift fall downwards, the hunters look just like—
—Shooting stars.
A sight that those who live in the deep seas rarely have the opportunity to witness.
With his desires and plans clouding his head, the bishop has, of course, never looked up at the night sky to enjoy that spectacle.
But they are different.

When the Abyssal Hunters had to fight for their lives and escape from the melancholy and sacrifice, they swam upwards, and when they finally reached the surface, they could only solemnly gaze upon the star-filled night sky...
The Hunters have all etched the destinies of these short-lived stars into their hearts.
Despite everything, the darkness can still be broken.
Now die.
Three shooting stars illuminate the deep passageway.
The bishop is horrified. But because it has neither lungs nor vocal tracts, this thundering sound is as close as it gets to a scream for its current body.

Amidst the debris and crumbling walls, the monster's bloated, dying form sprawls across much of the karst cave. Once emanating a strange, eerie beauty, its bizarre body now begins to stiffen in dread as it faces impending doom.

lines.st: The bishop is dead, and he will be forgotten completely.

lines.x: This is bad.
There's not a single living thing outside this city.
I don't think we're going to be able to restock our supplies here.
Anyone find anything?
Nothing on my end. The folks in the other parts of the city won't say a word either.
They react when they see food, but won't answer any of my questions.
Don't even bother asking them for supplies. I've been giving out bits of whatever rations I had on me.
Things were mostly the same for me. The only word that comes to mind when I look at these houses is 'miserable.'
Hard to imagine how the locals can even live here.
Aye, I've searched a few markets. Seems they're all long-abandoned.
Safe to say most people here haven't had a good meal in a long while.
Breaks my heart.
Yeah...
I followed someone to the coast, thinking I could find something there, but...
Hrrh... Hrrh...
He's just like the others, wandering aimlessly on the beach and sometimes digging through sand, as if there's food buried beneath.
Hungry...
Fins, shells, can eat.
Eat, eat.
Senshi lays down his axe and nimbly makes his way in front of the pair, opening up the bundles they are carrying.
Within are piles of finbeasts and shells scavenged from the beach, but from the foul odor they emit, it's clear they've begun to rot.
Though the two seem barely conscious, they begin to stuff the putrefying food into their mouths, overcome by some primal fear of Senshi stealing their meal.
Oh. That's rather...
That's... just sad...
Since there's nothing here that's of help, we should get out of here, quick.
Their customs are far too strange, and I don't think they're particularly friendly either...
This is just my guess.
The locals here haven't had a proper meal for too long. This led to them gradually growing numb to their surroundings.
If we make 'em something good to eat, maybe it'll bring them back to their senses, even if just a bit.
Seriously?
It's true. It's normal that you don't know this, but starvation and stress will slowly make you lose yourself.
Sounds like you're talking from experience.
If possible, I was hoping we could at least fill their bellies with a proper meal.
I don't mind the idea, but we need to find food for ourselves first. If not, we won't get very far. Right, Chilchuck?
Yeah, I suppose.
But let's make one thing clear. The reason we lost our supplies in the first place is because you all went crazy and wandered off in Sami, so don't expect me to do everything this time too!
Well, naturally.
Weary travelers, do you seek... sustenance?
Wait, you can understand us?
Yes.
Great! We—
Wait, Marcille. Don't you find it strange that he can when everyone else can't?
It is natural for you to be wary.
But fear not. I have come to offer you guidance.
Who the hell are you?
I am an itinerant priest, bringing the teachings of the sea to the masses.
What of you? You do not seem like Iberians. What brings you to Sal Viento?
We're looking to resupply for our journey.
I presume you have not made much progress on that?
No matter. Since we are here, we will not see you starve.
Yes. We are the church taking care of this city. All under our eaves are brethren.
Though you may be outsiders, if you are willing to follow our guidance, you too may join us.
We would not allow our own to go hungry.
(Sounds pretty shady...)
If we go with him to find food, won't this mean the townspeople can have a decent meal as well?
Don't say that like you're actually thinking of joining his kooky cult!
*cackle* The ocean's blessing is boundless. It will not mind sharing a sliver with you.
But, it is only for the strong and worthy.
What do you mean?
I bet it means he knows where the food is, but not everyone can make it out alive.
That is one way to interpret it.
I wouldn’t want to influence you into danger, it’s just—
Just tell us where it is. We're experienced adventurers. We can handle a little danger.
If you say so. Follow the coastline south. There will be a cave.
What you seek is within.
There's something fishy about that guy, Laios.
I know.
Then why'd you listen to him?
I have a hunch that whatever weird's going on in town is connected to the church he was talking about.
If we follow his 'guidance', it should lead us to something.
Besides, if there's an ambush waiting for us, I'm sure you'll notice beforehand!
Well, I guess you're right...
Hmm? That's—
A kraken! It's huge!
Laios, look out!
Calm down. I don't think it's moving any time soon.
Eh! It's already dead?
Wow! A kraken this huge... The church wasn't lying to us!
Look at its tentacles, its suckers, its sea anemone-like organs! This must be its... head?
I'd love to see what it looked like alive. It should be about the size of a tower and would've looked impressive.
Though it does taste rather nasty...
This isn't a kraken, though maybe they share a common ancestor.
My stomach's starting to hurt from going hungry...
Still, you shouldn't just eat things you know nothing about!
I agree, we shouldn't.
Eh?
Sea creatures can contain toxins after their death. Take crabs, for example. They can be contaminated by harmful bacteria, even if they haven't been dead for long.
If you really want to eat something...
We can see if there are any parasites still alive in there!
Gah! I shouldn't have gotten them started!
I've learned to just say nothing in times like these.
I do wonder though, who killed this giant creature? It must have been hard avoiding these tentacles...
Laios, hush. I hear something...
Marcille uses a stick to hoist a gooey, fluorescent... thing.
Eww... I feel kinda sick just looking at it.
It glows, even during the day... It's beautiful...
Absolutely not! I'm gonna throw it away!
What a waste... Say, what's that you threw it on?
What is that?
Ah!
Is something wrong?
Laios, watch your step!
Oh! Seagrass?
Careful, lad. I don't think it's a good idea to step on it.
Hmm? Now that you mention it... I do feel a bit numb all over.
Avoid the seagrass as best you can, guys!
You say that, but there aren't many other places we can stand.
It looks like all the plants in this cave were actually monsters, huh?
There's no doubt about it. We're in the kraken's lair.
Which means all these things coming out from deeper in the cave must've lived alongside it.
Which means...
Fresh ingredients!
Marcille, can you use magic on the seagrass? Set it on fire, or something?
Huh?
Are you crazy?! If we make a fire, wouldn't that be like shouting 'Hey, over here!' to all the monsters?
Yeah, which is exactly why we're doing it.
The seagrass is spreading out from the depths of the cave. If we chased them in, we'd be at a disadvantage.
Better to use a fire and let them come to us.
Sounds like you really thought this through.
Don't worry, we're not going to take them head on. You can wait outside. Once we've gotten enough ingredients, we'll get out.
And if something does happen, you can come to our rescue.
Hmph. Whatever.
He must be going through a rebellious phase.
On a separate note, haven't you considered the remote possibility that I might not play along?
Eh?
From his expression, Marcille can tell that he never even considered she wouldn't.
*sigh* Fine. Cover your ears.
Oh? That noise...
I presumed they would have exercised more caution... Who would have thought they had such confidence?
Heheh. No matter. The outcome will be the same.
The church has need of men.
Become one of us.
Then we shall bring revelation to all Iberians.
That should be the trap set.
Just need to wait for them to get enough ingredients and get out of there. Should be enough to buy them some time.
Man, Laios, sometimes you really do think of everything.
Hmm?
What's a treasure chest doing here?
Let me guess, this world has mimics too?
This one's barely even trying.
It's a beach! There's nothing here but sand! Who does it think it's trying to fool?
Hmm? Footsteps... I bet it's that weird guy.
They will become our brethren. Soon, soon, they will become something greater...
Tch, it's him, alright. I knew he was sending us to our deaths.
I've got an idea.
When the Inquisition comes, they too may have a taste.
A taste of what?
Why... Why haven't you gone inside?
I'm not much of a fighter myself, so I'm waiting outside.
...Is that so?
You mentioned the Inquisition. Who're they?
Our common enemy.
Oh, mind telling me more?
(It seems this Durin has no talent for combat. I suppose there's no harm in chatting with him.)
(With his build, where could he even run off to?)
I do not mind.
We might be seen talking here. How about we go chat by that chest?
Very well.
Let us begin with how the Inquisition have been fooling the people of Iberia—eh?
(Slams open) (Devours whole) (Clamps shut) (Sways contently)
You might call them your brethren, but you don't know the first thing about these monsters.
When you're in a party like ours, that'll cost you dearly.
*phew* That's all of them handled!
I thought there'd be a lot more.
If they were living alongside the kraken, most probably fled when it died.
The ones who killed it in the first place must have exquisite taste...
Think these monsters are edible, Senshi?
Well, I'm certainly not eating any! They're disgusting!
I think as long as we handle them carefully, we can turn them into healthy, delicious meals!
We're not that far from the city though, or particularly hidden. There's no reason the locals wouldn't know of this place.
If they won't eat any of this stuff, surely that means none of it's edible?
That mysterious priest must have sent us here to die!
Not necessarily.
These are monsters, after all. We didn't see any weapons in town, so maybe they can't hunt here, not that they won't.
Seafood's a good source of protein. If we can make a proper meal out of them, I'm sure it'll be filling.
NonononoNO!
There's nothing wrong with it, the way I see it.
I might not have cooked a kraken before, but I know how to handle fish, shellfish, prawns, and octopus.
Just a reminder that we don't have anything else to eat anyway. Other than whatever washes up on the beach.
I know, okay?!
This one's got a transparent head full of... some sort of sticky liquid? It surrounds the organs and this yellow thing...
Definitely can't eat the organs. Let's have a look at that yellow stuff and the liquid...
Really reminds me of an egg.
I'll try beating it in a bowl later.
Looks like the inside of an egg to me too.
You two do realize we're in the middle of taking care of the monsters, right? Can you save the cooking talk for later?
Don't say that! This is the best way to research monsters in general.
Have you noticed how you've already helped us cook these sea anemone monsters, Marcille?
Oh, you're right.
I think it's a bit burnt after getting hit by explosion magic.
Then there's this strange seagrass. It just withered up in the fire.
It's probably inedible, right? How about we hold off on trying it?
I think you kept the heat just right.
Doesn't seem like there's any more coming out from deeper in. Guessing they're afraid of us.
I think we've gathered enough monster parts for a feast. Let's go.
Huh. Chilchuck, why's there a treasure chest next to you?
And that man out cold over there... Is that the priest?
Yep. Thought he could talk us into walking to our deaths, so I decided I'd use this chest to teach him a lesson.
Wait, he was lying to us? We really did manage to get lots of seafood there, though.
And did you just say 'this guy'?
Yeah. *sigh* I figured this world was going to have mimics, too.
(Slams open) (Slapped by Chilchuck) (Unable to reach anything) (Shakes angrily)
Seems you've got it under control.
Well, it's pretty crappy at hiding itself.
Anyway, I assume you're going to want to see what this world's mimics taste like, so here, take it.
Wow, really?! You're the best!
(Whispers) Does this count as playing nice with him?
(Whispers) Probably.
(Whispers) You're a good egg, lad.
(Whispers) Shut up!
C'mon, let's go start a fire on the beach!
Let's start by opening up this one that looks like a sea anemone.
I was thinking earlier... It should be here... Yep, there it is! Its head is on the bottom.
Eh? That flower organ on the top isn't its head?
Let's cut it open and get a better look. The head's structured a lot like a starfish...
Peel back its skin a bit...
Oho, it really is all yellow inside! Every 'petal' seems to be the same. I think this is its reproductive organ.
Now I really don't feel like eating it.
I know, I know, I'm not gonna eat another sea creature raw.
Like I said, you need to handle that stuff carefully or you're definitely getting a stomachache!
But properly-made normal ceviche and sashimi are delicious. Remember that for next time!
Then, how do you properly prepare 'normal' raw food?
Hah! Told you not to say anything!
Well? Go on.
Add mustard, lemon slices, onion, coriander, and minced garlic to soy sauce. If you have fish sauce, add a dash if you'd like, then in goes the raw seafood. Mix well and leave to marinate.
Something along those lines.
Sounds great!
...But we don't have any soy sauce or mustard.
We do have this. I noticed half a box of... 'Emergency Sanity Potions' tucked away in a corner of the cave.
Says here: 'Contains mustard extract.' Might be some sort of seasoning.
...'Manufactured by Rhodes Island'?
A product of the place we're looking for. If this is here, we might be closer than we thought.
Might as well use it to give the meat some flavor.
Just need to cut these up into the right size, put them in a container, add in some 'Emergency Sanity Potion,' onion and minced garlic, then finally add the raw meat and mix it all together...
Here, try some.
Delicious!
As for everything else...
Let me see.
A big pile of roast seagrass.
About a dozen of those egg monsters.
Another dozen of the sea anemone ones.
And one mimic.
Yep, this should be plenty.
And there you have it.
It looks like a lot, but there's still plenty left over. Should be enough to feed the city folk.
It all smells amazing.
Hmm... Even though it's a monster meat feast, it actually looks pretty good once it's all cooked.
Do you really plan to share all this with the people in town? Seems to me like you guys are itching to dig in.
But they don't even understand us when we speak...
I've got a plan.
We start eating here, by the coast, which will attract those wandering the beach.
It's clear they still possess some sort of reason. We let them eat their fill, then they can share the rest with their friends.
So, just an excuse after all.
Hmm... The locals might not be too familiar with this style of cooking. I do think we should demonstrate that it's edible first.
You heard him. Eat up, everyone.
I want more seaweed alcohol!
Oh, you're such a glutton.
Because it's delicious!
Well, I'm glad you like it.
I hope the singer will like it too, the next time she stops by.
What's wrong?
The outsiders are at the beach.
Huh? The beach?
What are they doing there?
Cooking.
Cooking?
With monsters from the sea.
What?! I need to go stop them, quick!
Too late.
This ceviche is really sharp.
Texture's good though. Haven't had anything like it before.
This world's mimics turned out to be pretty tasty too.
Kin... We many... What's that smell? It's good...
Oh. That lying priest's woken up.
So very good... Wait, what are you doing?! You... What the hell is that you're cooking?!
Oh, hey there, Mister Priest. That cave you told us about really was full of seafood!
The townspeople don't need to go hungry any longer!
Want to try some?
You... You... This is blasphemy! Blasphemy of the highest order!
M-My brethren, I will not allow—
Oh dear. Looks like he's passed out again.
Maybe he's hungry?
I've got it! We can gather up some of that strange seagrass and use the tingle to wake him up!
I already told you, he tried to get us killed! Why do you care about him so much?
No matter how you cut it, Chilchuck, we're only having this feast thanks to him.
We ought to show our gratitude somehow.
I really don't get you.
Alright, come help me gather some seagrass while the bonfire's still lit!
Hey, you're just doing this because you're still hungry!
Augh, the water's so cold...
Anita finally reaches the shore, but it seems she is too late.
Upon a beach fallen into silence, people have gathered for the first time in a long while.
A group of four have rolled up their trousers and waded into the sea in high spirits, breaking out into laughter on occasion.
Wow! There's creatures here nibbling at my toes!
Huh? Th-Then doesn't that mean the stuff we just ate was...
That's just how things are in most places, Marcille.
I think I hear people yelling behind us.
Really?
Oh wow, there's some glowing round thing over there! I'm gonna go have a look!
Ahh! Ahhhhh!!
They—! Th-th-th-they—!
AHHHHHHHH!
All Anita can think of while looking at the scene before her is that...
It seems the sea breeze blowing towards Sal Viento today is stinging her eyes. All this is... something she never imagined she'd see her entire life.
What's going on?! Why are we being chased by all these magic items?! Who sent them after us?!
My sword can't reach them! They're not like the other monsters we've fought before!
Marcille! Use your magic already!
I... I got caught in the blast!
*cough* *cough*! They can use both explosion and freezing magic, and target them with precision. These things sure are something!
They're too quick, I can't aim like this!
What do we do then?!
We run! I'll use my magic to counter theirs! Try to get out of their range!
Looks like there's a village that way! These things flew in over from there though, so I don't think we're going to see any friendly faces...
Marcille! Look out!
I figured out their flight patterns!
Leave us... alone already!
The bombs are sent flying by Marcille's magic, heading right back to the drones that fired them.
Hoist by their own petard, they rattle and shake as they fall to the ground.

With a massive boom, the buildings are set ablaze, and the ringing of the blast is closely followed by the shouting of the villagers. After the explosion comes the falling magic items, which careen into a few unlucky souls.
Marcille can't help but tighten her grip on her staff.
I think we made them really mad, Laios!
Senshi, Marcille, sometimes we just have to grit our teeth and get fighting.
A shame... we still haven't found out anything about Rhodes Island...
By golly, the Catastrophe didn't ruin none of our corn fields! Didn't get licked by the sandstorm neither!
I've been worried sick for days over this. If the fields're gone, we'd have nothing to eat or sell past summertime!
How bout's I go start up the drones then, boss? Other places mightn't have been as lucky. If they see we're still spick and span, we're gonna have a problem with thieves and looters.
Well go on then. Just 'member to switch them from patrol mode to attack mode.
Oh right, remember to let that Rhodes Island fella in, he's meant to be stopping by after the Catastrophe!
You got it, boss!
Eh? That ain't right... Why's the drone relay all...
The drones ain't responding? Signal's... Boss! Boss!!!
Th-The Catastrophe hit the drone relay! The drones— They're outta control!
Alright, don't lose your cool! Turn that relay off first!
I can't! It ain't shutting down!
Gosh dang diddly darn it! Figure it out yourself! I'm not paying you to sit around and do nothing!
Ah! The, the... Originium circuits... The mainline board...
Hey, what's going on here?! Not gonna welcome me in?
No, no, you're a sight for sore eyes! I remember you, you're Ryan, the guy with the little hammer! Come help us out, the drones are outta control!
That so? Alright, hang back a bit. I'll go take a look for you—
Wha— Who was that?! Is someone really here to take our crops?!
Screw those bastards!
Hey, this is Ryan, with a new entry for the mission log.
First, bringing back Ceobe. Uh... This mission's proving a bit harder than anticipated. Our intel says this is the last place the kid showed up, but actually finding her's not so simple.
Then I've got to pay a visit to the 'Cornhole', see how they're doing after the Catastrophe. Hope everything's alright there—
What was that?! Is there a dust-up going on in the frontier?
Ah! The, the... Originium circuits... The mainline board...
(...I don't see anyone from outside the village? Seems like they've got some kind of internal issues.)
(Guess I better find out what's going on. These guys aren't the friendliest bunch...)
Hey, what's going on here?! Not gonna welcome me in?
No, no, you're a sight for sore eyes! I remember you, you're Ryan, the guy with the little hammer! Come help us out, the drones are outta control!
(Phew, okay, just some out-of-control drones. If there really was fighting out here, I'd be done for...)
That so? Alright, hang back a bit. I'll go take a look for you—
*hack* *cough* *cough* *cough*!!!
(Dammit, there really are other pioneers here to pick a fight!)
Wha— Who was that?! Is someone really here to take our crops?!
Screw those bastards!
(This can't be good. if I get Rhodes Island involved in a frontier tussle, we'll be in trouble. L-Let's get these guys to safety first, and hopefully that'll calm things down—)
Hey, don't be rash, buddy! You don't know how many people are out there, we best hunker down for now!
Frick that, I saw four of 'em earlier! Go for the two shorties first!
Let's gettem!
Hey, wai—
...Eh? Only four of them? What kind of gang is this?
Two groups? Be careful, they've got weapons, and more of those magic items Marcille can't do anything about. Just stay calm.
CALM?! I AM DONE BEING CALM! My hair's all messed up thanks to that explosion! Ruined my braid! I really liked it, you know?!
You ess-oh-bees think you can mess with me? You sick freaks think you can just bomb my barn?!
No, stop! Let's talk it out like civilized folk! The drones are still flying around like crazy! Do you really want to pick a fight now?
Shove it! You think I'm scared of my own drones?
What good is talking going to do when it's them who attacked us out of nowhere?!
Shut it, lady, you're the ones who blew up my barn! You guys got a death wish—
They're attacking! Take cover!
Well, yeah, they're the ones controlling the magic items!
Gah! Yowch! None of these pains-in-the-butt are listening to me— Ow!
Look, fighting out in the wild's just going to draw in who-knows-what! Let's all calm down and talk things out, okay?
As though in response to his earnest plea, all of a sudden, the ground begins to rumble.
Could it be another Catastrophe? The thought flits through the minds of some, but is immediately dispelled by what comes next.
A tidal wave of gloompincers seems to have had enough of the drones dropping bombs in the skies above their heads. They raise their pincers in formation and march towards the gaggle of people...
A swarm of monsters?!
Run! We can't handle that many with just the four of us!

But there's nowhere to run! We're surrounded!
Let's just back off for now...
Ugh!
—Gah! Watch where you're going! Who was that?! I'll hammer you into—It's you punks?!
We can't run, boss! The gloompincers got us hemmed in!
Everyone, please, listen to me. We'll deal with them together, alright?
A-And don't forget the drones!
I-Is it over? Did we manage to take care of them all?
Hah... Hah... I'm beat...
Hahaha! Not bad, girlie! You've got quite a few tricks up your sleeve. I sure am a big fan of casters!
Oh... Good... They aren't at fisticuffs any longer... Good, good, as long as everyone's fine...
C'mon, let's go, I'm in a good mood today! You can have a taste of some of my corn!
Boss, you...
Hmm? ...Wait, corn...
The laughter dies on the pioneer's lips as he raises his weapon at the team of four.
*huff*... *huff*... Guess he remembered. And here I was thinking we could bury the hatchet.
Marcille raises her staff, strands of her hair rippling with magic.
Wha? Are we back at this already? Really? You were all battle buddies earlier, surely that's got to count for something, right?
Hmph!
*huff*... *huff*...
Let's not go for magic right away, Marcille.
We're all tired out from dealing with those things. There's no benefit in starting up another fight.
We'll count down from three, lower our weapons, go back the way we came, and agree not to bother each other for the night. How about that?
*cough* *cough* *cough*! How... about...... that...........?
Look, this guy's plenty sincere, alright? He's still offering even after he got a bomb chucked at him! Why not think his offer over?
Our boys are tuckered out too, boss. I don't think fighting'll do us any good neither.
...Fine. We'll get out of each other's hair for the night. Start counting.
Oh, yes, good!
Three, two, one!
Great... I can sleep soundly tonight...
Zzz...
Marcille? Marcille?
Zzz... Eh...?
Ah! Wh-What's going on now?!
Nothing.
We were thinking of going to examine the bodies of those monsters earlier, and while we're at it...
...Huh?
Well, they looked like crabs to me, so they might taste like mimics.
Ahh, the chewiness of mimics... The more you chew it, the better the flavor gets.
Just don't use my lockpicks to scoop the meat out again.
But they've got just the right shape for it!
'Bob's Gourmet Guide' says they're edible. Apparently they're delicious, as long as you can get through the shell! Don't you want to have a taste?

Absolutely not! Besides, if those villagers think we're sneaking back onto the battlefield for an ambush, we might get into another fight!
Those magic items flying all over the place were a pain to deal with, and there's still plenty of them out there! Not to mention, I'm still tired as—
Huh?
Munchmunchmunch... Huh?
Munch... Huh?
Huh???
The pioneers freeze in the middle of stuffing gloompincer legs into their mouths.
The only sound breaking the awkward silence is the crackling of the bonfire.
The sparks plik and plak amidst the flames.
Their pincers and limbs are stuffed full of flesh. No wonder they can support such heavy shells, as well as crush those magic items...
So you cut them open from the belly, then pull back their shell... I see now. Looks like you prepare these things the same way you do mimics.
Then... Oooh...
That smells amazing!
Err... Wanna try some?
...Ggh.
Laios accepts the roasted meat, hesitates for a moment, then stuffs it into his mouth.
IT'S DELICIOUS!
Haha! It's all in the technique—*cough*! Gah...
Want a taste?
...Eh? It really is chewy! So fragrant too!
Here, you guys have some as well.
Huh? For me? Thanks...
It's roasted just right, so it hasn't lost any moisture. It even tastes just like mimic!
Alright, just eat already.
Keep your lips shut once you're done, I've got a reputation to keep.
Not a word about tonight, you hear?
(How is it you can be at each others' throats during the day, but be eating roast gloompincer together at night?! Guess there wasn't any need for me to rush over...)
Just going for a midnight tinkle, don't mind me, haha.
Gosh darnit... C'mon over! You've given us meds, and I never forget a debt.
I'll let you guys off this time...
...No, I can't! My barn! Why'd you guys blow up my barn?!
We didn't! It was your magic items that attacked us! We had to counter their magic in self-defense!
M-Magic items? You mean my drones? They went nuts, weren't listening to me!
Huh?! But you still came out and attacked us!
Because you were gonna steal our crops!
You thought we were what—
Alright, alright, I've heard enough, I get what happened now! This was all one big misunderstanding!

Your drones went amok and bombed them by accident, they thought you were attacking them, then you thought they were trying to take your stuff using the Catastrophe as cover. One! Big! Misunderstanding!
So, how about we all shake hands and let bygones be bygones?
No.
Forget it.
I'm... frozen...stiff...!
How...about....we...take...care...of...them...first...?
The drone relay's busted. I took a couple of courses on equipment maintenance, let me see if there's a way to fix it...
Gah! Anyone understand Originium circuits enough to come help me out? Everyone else handle the drones, get them to use up their bombs! I can't get anything done while I'm getting pelted by them!
Go on then, we'll draw them away! Y'all work quick, ya hear!
Aaaaaaaahhhhhhhh––––
Only thing I can do is help them remove the cover of this 'drone relay' quicker! Marcille, do you seriously have no way to handle those things? We can't ask Senshi to boil them all!
Uh... Umm... You'd need to give me time to learn a whole new system of magic...
Two more magic items flying over! Their flight speeds follow a pattern, and so do their patrol routes...
Each one carries a single bomb. Seems they fly away as soon as they drop it. Are they returning to their nest to replenish their stock?
I can't think of anything on such short notice...
I've got a plan.
What is it?
You think these items use some magic or other to know who's an enemy?
Well, they're not monsters. They don't have eyes, but since they can only throw a bomb once, they must have some way to tell friend from foe...?
Right, lad. I noticed earlier that they didn't pay any attention to a dead gloompincer.
But then I saw a live one walk into one's range by accident. At first, no response, just like the dead one, but the moment the gloompincer raised its pincers, it got a bomb thrown at it.
...So they detect 'hostility'? The moment they sense it, they attack?
Oh, I've got a way to make them use up their bombs then!
What is it?
I'll use a familiar to act as bait!
A familiar?
Nothing too complex, I just need somethings full of hostility. I can use the gloompincers from earlier!
Get me some fruit from the forest to add in as well. Just pile them up while I think of what I want to make it look like...
Whoa whoa whoa, why're you cutting your hair all of a sudden?
Hair's full of magical power. It's a good resource to have on hand.
Some flour, a handful of sugar, then knead in some butter... Now I just need a reference from real life.
Hmm? Why're you looking at me like that?
Ah, I know what I want to use as the model!
(Incants)...
Is it done? What's it look like? Let me see!
Hey, isn't that me?!
Alright, time for a test run...
Left leg, right leg, left leg, right leg...
(Follows her commands)
Okay, now run!
Good, let's get going!
AHHH! AHHHHHHHHH!!!!
Have you repaired it yet?! We're on our last legs here!
We're here!
With a cheer, the pioneers turn their heads, but all they see is a strange figure running towards them at full speed, shouting in a stiff voice they don't recognize.

They have faced bloodthirsty wild beasts, survived Catastrophes big and small, and have been pelted by barrages of bombs from dozens of drones, but the sight is still enough to make the pioneers tremble in fear.
A man down on all fours, his long tongue rolling from his slobbering maw, his eyes bulging, screech as he crawls towards them at full speed!

Left leg! Right leg! Left leg! Right leg! Gah...
Why did I make it move that way?!?
(Follows her commands)
...AHHHHHH! RUN FOR IT!!

Just get back as fast as you can! I'll distract the magic items! Don't stop here, head back to—
Eh? Where'd everyone go?
...! The items must have noticed us!
I just need to lead them somewhere far from everyone else, then get them to throw all their bombs at the familiar!
There's a field here, that should be okay...
Ah! I nearly forgot! They're not going to wait for my command to start attacking! They've already begun!
No good, it's still too close; I'll get hit too. I need to find somewhere a bit further away!
It's working! Marcille got the drones to head over there!
Now they just need to use up all their bombs! She's amazing!
Your 'magic' sure is real weir—I mean, great.
Argh! This is way more difficult than I imagined!
*pant*... *pant*...
Wait, that noise...?
They're about to fire?!
This place... Would here be alright? Are we far enough? I need to keep running! It's all up to you now, Fami-Laios! I'm releasing control over you!
Gurgh...
Hey, I see her running out into the open! The drones are firing!
Hooray! She's done it!
That girlie sure is something... I always said I was a big fan of casters.
Yeah, Marcille's amazing.
Wait, I just remembered something...
Isn't that where the corn fields are?
What?!
I think you might be right...
Hey, wait! MY... MY CORN FIELDS!!!
They're about to fire!
I'll hide here—Whoa!
Explosions resound all around her, as expected.
But they are joined by the sweet scent of corn wafting through the air.
Marcille raises her head. It's like time itself has slowed, to the point that she can see every kernel fly through the sky, popping into fragrant popcorn of purest white under the bombardment.
The popcorn falls, floats, flutters like snow, landing on the tip of her nose.
She shudders.
...Huh?
Before she can do anything else, she is buried in an avalanche of fluffy white popcorn.
Gurgh...
Th-This is...
Popcorn!
I'm super strong! Even with a hundred... Err, wait, ninety-nine pieces of popcorn in front of me, I can eat them all!
Hyah! Bam! Pow!
Mount Popcorn, here I come!
Marcille! Marcille?
Marcille! Where are you? Can you hear us?
Oh no... We can't resurrect Marcille here!
Munchmunchmunch...
Hmm, that sounds familiar...
That's the sound of Kay eating!
Munchmunchmunch... Oh! Mister Beard! It's you guys!
Why are you here, lass?!
I smelled something real tasty, so I ran over!
Come on, Mister Beard, Mister Short, Mister Blond! There's so much popcorn and frozen fruit to eat!
Munchmunchmunch... Oh yeah... munchmunchmunch... you're here too, Miss Blonde!
Don't...
Don't... eat... my... HAIR!
Pfweh! Sorry! I didn't mean to!
Marcille! You're alive!
*cough* *cough*... Just buried, not bombed.
Wow! This sticky stuff smells great!
Brr, it's all frozen! Real different from the popcorn!
*sigh*... Sure is...
Want to try some?
Munchmunch... So frosty and sweet! Yummy!
Oh thank heavens, I'm glad you're alright!
Eh? ...Kay?!
Huh? Oh, I remember you!
You were the one who came hunting with me! Did you get anything? I got looooots of stuff, but I ate it all!
You...
Never mind. The guys before me were right. As long as I find you, it's mission complete—Hey, wait, what're you eating?! Spit that out!
Munchmunchmunch!
Don't just eat stuff off the ground! C'mon, spit it out already!
Munchmunchmunch... *gulp*!
AHHHH!!!
Don't worry, she's just eating popcorn.
Heavens to Betsy! What doggone disaster! That corn was gonna go to market!
Boss, don't say that, you're a Perro yourself...
How'm I supposed to sell any of this after it's been wrecked like this?!
I mean, you can sell popcorn. There's definitely people willing to buy it, out by the tents.
Throw in some butter and sugar, warm it up a bit while giving it a stir... Good, the smell's coming through! Not bad for a snack.
Pound the frozen fruit, stir in some batter from the familiar, shape it for presentation's sake... and you've got ice cream!
There you have it, a sweet treat!
Eh... Hmm... I guess this popcorn's good enough to sell...
Not like I got much choice now...
I'll have a bite, then...
*cough* *cough*...
Not yet recovered?
Just breathed in a bit too much corn smell all at once.
It's really delicious, though.
The popcorn's warm and buttery, sweet and crisp...
The ice cream's amazing, too. The fruit's so fresh, and with such a smooth texture...
Best of all, no monster meat.
Mhm. Lucky me.
*phew*... Glad that's all done and dusted.
Mmm... Heheh... Yummy, yummy ice cream...
*sigh* You sure know how to make people worry, kid.
Hey, I'll be taking her home now. You guys don't start fighting once I'm gone, alright?
Yeah, yeah, I know.
Ryan helps up Ceobe, fast asleep on the ground after eating her fill, and takes her to the nearby vehicle.
Its engine roars to life, and putters away into the distance.
Hmm? Where's Kay gone?
You mean that other girlie? Ryan's taken her home.
Ryan? Why?
Sounded like he was on some sort of mission for Rhodes Island.
Wait, did you say Rhodes Island?! Ryan's from Rhodes Island?!
Yeah. Why? What's got you in such a tizzy?
Laios!
Does that mean it's close by?
Uh, yeah, they're stopped a few kilometers away.
Ryan just left. If we start running now, we can catch up.
Let's go then! We can't afford to lose their trail again!
Right.
...Huh?
What're they on about?
Let's review all the information we have on Rhodes Island so far.
First, both Big Bob and the High Priest thought they could help us...
Second, Kay said there's someone called the 'Doctor' who can definitely solve our problem...
And lastly, it seems we're finally getting close to their ship!
I just hope they really do have a way of solving this...
Hey guys, look!
The three head over to the cliff's edge at Chilchuck's cry. It is then they realize why there was a tinge of worry in his voice.
They see a giant, colossal ship.
It sails across the land, a vessel much larger than any seafaring ship they have seen before.
A massive construct of steel. A city. A... dungeon.
This is it. Rhodes Island.
...My word, a ship that sails on land.
Can we really find a way home there?
I think we've got another problem to deal with first.
How are we going to get on board?
I can't see any entrances, even from here.
Look! I think there's some people approaching the ship.
Looks like a caravan to me.
A caravan, huh...
Why don't we go talk to them and see if they can bring us aboard?
We're back.
Good work out there.
No problem. Oh, right. We just ran into a party of four looking for help. They're dressed real funny, said they know Ceobe, and were hoping Rhodes Island could help them go home.
Hm? Go home?
More or less, from what I understand. Would you mind leading the way for them? I'll go talk with Field Operations.
Hello!
If you came to us for aid, you must be looking for the Doctor or Amiya, right?
Err... Kay did mention a 'Doctor,' so they're probably who we're looking for?
Could you set up a meeting for us?
I have to check your identities first. If you could just follow me—
Whoa! Really sorry, dropped my things...
'Bob's Gourmet Guide'?
You know Big Bob?
Sure do. He cooked us the first meal we had here.
Aye, anyone who can make such a delicious meal out of such hard bugs is admirable in my eyes.
I see. So you're the 'friends' he mentioned.
Oh?
I'm Mudrock. Big Bob told me about you. At the time, he had just decided to leave the mercenary life behind, but had no idea whether starting a farm would be viable.
Farms in the wilderness are often targeted by bandits and looters. He didn't have many business connections, and he wasn't sure if the beer he brewed was any good.
But the joy on your faces as you ate his cooking gave him confidence.
I never realized he was in such dire straits.
Is... Is he alright nowadays?
Don't know. We haven't met in a long while.
All I've heard is that his Originium slug beer factory's doing well and making a profit. Seems he's got it made.
Good, good.
But I also heard that he broke some local laws in the process. Some kind of tax issue. He was sent to jail.
Jail? Are you sure he's still alright?
The most recent news I heard was that he either broke out or made bail. Either way, he's fine.
I guess that's... good?
A jailbreak, huh...
I've also heard that he set up a grocery store in Sami. Business is good there, apparently.
Oh...
He... knows how to persevere, at least.
Anyway, come with me. We'll head up in the elevator.
'Elevator'? You mean this iron box? How do you use it?
Looks like another magic item to me...
Go on inside. We're heading to the first floor. Wait a moment though, I need to get a—
Hmm?
...Where did they go?
Why's everything all different now?!
Where are we? What just happened?
This place is looking more and more like a dungeon by the second.
...As I have foreseen.
Hey, aren't you the Cyclops from Sami?
A Cyclops?! How do you know—
Wait. If you're here, that means you saw something, right?
Indeed.
Then, what did you see?
On your road ahead, there will be flames, a great shock... and a fearsome beast.
Do you know where Ceobe or the Doctor are?
Ceobe has just returned. She should be at the training room now, which is that way.
As for the Doctor... Please forgive me for not being able to tell you anything at the moment.
Thanks.
So... where should we go first?
I don't think Mudrock will find us any time soon.
This is the training room the lady from earlier told us about.
Doesn't seem like there's anyone here. There's some great big strange thing over there...
What are you lot doing here with that gormless look on your faces?!
Eh? High Priest?!
Have you seen Ceobe, High Priest?
She was here a moment ago, but ran off somewhere.
You guys looking for her?
Yeah!
Don't just stand there then, get your butts over here and sit in this machine!
Huh? Uh... Sure, okay.
High Priest, did you happen to see some lost visitors? We heard they were spotted around here.
What? No, I haven't. We're about to run an impact safety test on this new model of Big Ugly.
I suggest you stand back a bit.
Uh... Sure.
Alright, Zumama, everything's ready to go!
Are you sure?
Of course I'm sure.
AAAAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHHH—!
Was it just me or were there people inside that thing when it got blasted?
Are you sure about that? Did you see anyone get blasted?
Oh, huh... Guess not?
Just my imagination, then.
Yeah, yeah, go look somewhere else. The ones you're looking for aren't here.
Sure. Thanks.
I think this is what they call an 'abuse of power.'
They put me in a stew one time, I blew them up one time. Fair's fair.
Where'd you send them?
They wanted to find Ceobe, so I sent them to her.
That part's no lie.
The smoke from the explosion clears, and the four find themselves experiencing the oddly-familiar feeling of floating in midair. As they regain their senses, gravity begins to do its work.
Surprisingly, it is P'o-shan Village rushing up to meet them. Dawn watches them with a peculiar expression on her face.
AHHHHHHH!!! HELP US—
The four flail about in the air, to no avail.
However, it does not escape their notice that below them is not solid ground, but a near-infinitely deep well.
Instead of a cold, hard, and most-likely-fatal landing, they find themselves shooting straight into their hole of hope.
...A tad crude, but I guess that's how she will send them on their way.
May we meet again someday.
Did Kay run into the kitchen again?
That she has.
But she's been acting oddly this time around.
How so?
She's been unusually silent since her return.
I'll go have a chat with her, then.
Kay.
Hmm?
Are you alright? Do you feel unwell?
Uhh... Dunno know how to describe it...
I've been feeling weird ever since I got back to the ship...
If that's the case, why don't we go to Medical together and let the doctors have a look?
...Don't wanna.
Why not?
I dunno why, but I feel like... I should wait here by myself.
Oh. Alright then. Go get some rest, okay?
Yeah.
Huh?!
Oww... My butt...
We're in... a kitchen?
Who are you?!
Get behind me, Amiya!
Be careful, everyone!
Looks like High Priest sent us somewhere where they're not too friendly.
High Priest? Who are you—
What was that?
It came from the room Kay's resting in!
Let's go take a look, quick!
What's going on?
They mentioned Kay, we should follow them!
What... What in the world?
Is that you, Kay?
My tummy...
What is going on here? Is this really the Kay we know?
Grr...
I can tell from her emotions that she's still Kay, the one we all know and love.
She's still holding on, but she's about to lose control!
Is... Is this really Kay?
(Howls)
What a handsome hound! I didn't know you could do this kind of thing, Kay!
Is this really the time for that?!
We need to get her to her senses!
Guests.
Based on what you just said, I suppose you came out of concern for Kay's condition, correct?
Well, we came because we wanted to find a way back to our world... but we're still really worried for her!
Then let's save the questions for later and get her under control together!
If we can't, then I'm afraid things will get far, far out of hand.
Laios?
Sure. We'll work together, for now.
(Howl of anger)
What magic should I use here... It feels like no matter what I go for, it'll hurt Kay—
Please, help me keep her still! I'll calm her down!
Easy for you to say, but she's being a real terror without a leash!
Senshi, do we still have any of that popcorn left over?
Aye.
Try throwing some of it for her to eat!
Got it!
Before he even finishes speaking, Senshi reaches into his sack for a ball of popcorn and lobs it at Ceobe.
Ceobe stares at it as if entranced, her anger placated.
I knew it! She's looking for something to eat!
Maybe something she ate on her journey upset her stomach and made her this way!
Since when do people turn into monsters when they get a stomachache?
Dr. Kal'tsit once told me that Kay comes from a special bloodline, so that might actually be possible.
Are you serious?!
Save the talk for later! Let's get her under control while she's distracted!
You, with the sword! You, with the pot on your back! You two look strong; let's hold her down together.
Right!
The three of them sneak behind Ceobe while she happily munches on popcorn. With all their might, they each grab one of her legs firmly.
(Unhappy howling)
Now, Amiya!
Got it!
Come back to us, Kay.
Don't be scared. You're safe at home right now.
(Pained howl)
Amazing... I've never seen magic like that before.
Look, Kay's back!
Oh, thank goodness!
Kay! are you alright?
Hmm...? Wha... what happened...?
It's like I was... dreaming!
Dreaming?!
Yeah...
Ceobe looks around her. The once neat-and-tidy room is now in utter shambles. Her hand is still curled around half a broken toy.
I'm sorry... Vulcan told me if I do something bad, I have to say sorry, and it looks like I did something bad...
I was real hungry in my dream, and my tummy kept rumbling... and then...
And then...?
And then I grew lots of heads, and I wanted to eat a whole bunch of things!
All I had to do was eat until I was full, then I'd be real happy, and I'd come back here!
(Whispers) Matterhorn, where's Vulcan? Has she noticed anything about Kay recently?
(Whispers) I'll go ask her once we're done here.
(Whispers) So, what should we do with her now?
...*rumble*...
Oh? Hungry again?
Yep!
I wanna eat lots and lots!
I was full in my dream, but now I'm hungry again!
Hmm...
Kay, do you know of a monster with three heads?
Of course I know!
What do you know about it?
It doesn't just have three heads, it's got a thousand!
Every time I wanna eat lots and lots, I grow ten, a hundred... no, a thousand heads! That's why I can never get enough!
I've got a thousand heads!
You what?!
Well, as long as my tummy's full, I won't become like how I was just now!
Well then! I'll cook you up a proper meal right away!
We'll make an exception to the 'no snacks before a meal' rule. We do need to bring her in for a medical exam, though.
Now, friends from afar, why don't you take a stroll around Rhodes Island? This might be a bit much to ask, but I was hoping you could show off your cooking skills once more at mealtime.
S-Sure, thanks...
As for the other reason you sought us out...
We might need some time to know your circumstances before we can figure out how to get you home.
Oh... So, what do we do now?
I wouldn't worry too much. The Doctor and Dr. Kal'tsit might be able to find a way.
Really?!
Yes. Whatever the case, you did help Kay, and your situation is... rather peculiar.
If you like, you can stay on Rhodes Island for a while.
As our representative, allow me to welcome you all aboard.
More newbies? Which department are they under?
Weird, can't tell what race they are. They hiding their tails or something?
I saw a young lady carrying a whole lot of crops just now. Mind showing me your farms here?
Farms... Do you mean Horticulture and Hydroponics, that kind of thing? Follow me.
You guys got an inn here?
Oh, so you're a Durin! C'mon, it's this way.
Do you... do you have anywhere I could take a proper bath and comb my hair?
Of course, dear, the dorms have all you need. Just follow me!
Oh, such long rabbit ears! I wasn't able to get a good look at their capillary distribution last time. What a shame...
Wow, they can adjust the position of their tails to maintain their balance. Ingenious...
Hmm... Some clearly have feathers, but they have no wings. Can they fly?
Ah, this place really is heaven...
Oh, isn't he one of the new operators they were talking about? He looks a bit lost, standing in the middle of the corridor like that. Do you think he's too embarrassed to ask for directions?
Maybe he's a bit shy. Why don't we start the conversation?
Hello! Do you happen to be—
(Whispers) Do these birdmen lay eggs? If they're unfertilized, then can I see how one tastes? With this many people on board, surely I can get a few...
(Whispers) Can goatman hair be knitted into sweaters? The ones I saw earlier had bare arms though, doesn't seem like they grow wool. A shame...

(Whispers) But what I really want to know is, if a catman and a dogman have a child, would they be a cat or a dog? Or maybe they're half-cat half-dog? ...Maybe the top is a cat and the bottom is a dog? Or maybe it's split left and right instead?
(Whispers) ...What if it's with a birdman? Would they lay an egg that hatches into a cat? Hmm...
Yeah...
Uh...
(Collectively step back)
Oh, hello.
(Collectively take another step back)
Can I ask you a question...
(Collectively take yet another step back)
Do you lay eggs?

opts: He wasn't asking you, Mon3tr. No need to come out.

lines.x: Hmm? Oh! Sorry, I was asking those bird—err, Liberi... Huh? Where'd they go?
Why's everyone standing so far away from me?
Hmm?
Amiya! That's him! That's the guy I was telling you about!
He's no patient, he's one of those... 'guests' we found waking up on the deck!
He-he-he's been asking us all sorts of dreadful questions, over and over again!
Ah... What sort of questions?
He asked us if our children would be half-cat half-dog!
He asked if Liberi could lay eggs, and if he could eat them!
(Hiss)
Oh, my, what a magnificent one. You're called Mon3tr, right?
Can I touch you?
(Piercing cry)
(Nimble motion)
Wh-Whoa?!
Wow! How do you keep your hair so golden and shiny when you spend most of your time in a dangerous dungeon? Without any good haircare either!
Heheh! It's all because of how important hair is to a magician. I store some of my mana in it.
Here. This is one of my favorite hair creams. It has perfume and herbal extracts mixed in, and it'll keep your hair moisturized and protected.
The only reason my hair is as sleek as it is now is thanks to frequent application!
Wow, it smells great!
If you'd like a change of pace in your hairstyle, I've got lots of braiding tips I can show you!
Where. Do. You. Think. You're. Going?
I was just—
Whoa!
What were you doing with my flames?
Uh...
I said, what were you doing with my flames?!
...Roasting some chestnuts...

......
Roasting.
Some.
Chestnuts!
Roasting some chestnuts.
The lass driving the tractor gave me a bushel of raw ones, said they were the first harvest of chestnuts she managed to grow on Rhodes Island.
You manage your farms well. Chestnuts make a mighty fine snack. They get soft and sweet after roasting, and their shells become easy to peel too.

Pity the fire coming off your body was too hot! Ended up burning 'em all. I said I'd cook a meal for you, but I never expected to waste perfectly good ingredients like that. Real shameful behavior, if you ask me...
Skyfire, please calm down!
Ahh! Shaw, Shaw! Come quick! Skyfire's catching fire again!
Mister, please don't cook anything else on her, especially not those kebabs!
*gulp*!
Not half bad.
Phew. Anyway, like I said, I'm not a Durin.
Wait, you're not?
You're not a child then, are you?
Mind sitting down when we talk? I can't see your face.
I am sitting down.
Care for another?
My thanks.
Are all the men here this tall? Even Laios seems a bit short compared to you guys...
(Whispers) This guy covered in silvery-white hair... If Laios saw him, I bet the only thing he'd be thinking about is asking him if he'd like some help combing it all...
Ah, whatever.
A drinking party? Without me?
I know it's been a while, but did all of you really forget to ask me?
Hmm? Hey, what's with the kid? Kids shouldn't be drinking.
Uhh...
*gulp*
*sniffsniffsniff*
(Something smells really good!)
(Maybe they finished cooking?)
(Tasty food's here!!)
Let's gooooo!
My back—
(Uh-oh, I hit someone...)
Sorry!
Vulcan told me if I ran into someone, I have to say sorry.
Sorry, Mister Beard! I ran into you 'cause I was hungry!
It's fine, come help me put it all on the table!
A meal made with the ingredients and memories given to us by the folks we met along the way. 'Tis done!
Come have a bite. Do they all taste like they did before?
Hmm... I wanted to ask earlier while we were preparing the ingredients, but why are we making the exact same dishes we had on our journey?
Does repeating the same recipe make you feel better?
...That, I can't say for sure. But if it's got the same taste, then I'll think back to the first time I had it, and what was going through my mind at the time.
Like if I said 'scorpion hotpot,' what's the first thought that comes to mind, Marcille?
...The first time I met you in the Beginners' Plaza.
Walking mushroom feet are both edible and tasty, with a unique aroma.
Scorpions are a pain to cut into bits.
What about that basilisk?
You saying you had to cook the antidote herb with the chicken for that adventurer so it'd taste better...
Basilisks are really cool!
Marcille finally getting to eat that chicken she was craving.
Then, mandrake?
Very unique heads!
Marcille and that big bat—
We can skip over that one, okay?!
See? That's what I mean.
Food is connected to memory. When you eat something you've had before, your brain automatically goes back to the other times you've had it.

It's the same when I cook. I think of the people I first made the dish with. Why did we choose to make it? Did we learn anything new while preparing the ingredients? Any happy smiles after we were done eating?
This way, the aroma and flavor can recreate the scene in your mind, bit by bit.
As for the meal we're about to have? That gets to be a new memory, to be brought up during future dinners.
Wow...
You've always had a way with words, Senshi.
Alright, enough prattle! Let's eat!
Chow time!
Mmm. These carrots are perfect! Would you like to try some, Doctor?
Are you sure you don't want any roast Originium slug? Really? It's delicious.
Not even one shell?
Wow... Can I really eat this?
Of course!
All of it?
All of it!
Yippee! You are so kind!
Oh, right... 'Doctor,' was it? Just noticed you there.
While we were cooking, the operators told me you're often so busy with work that you forget to eat.
And when you do remember, it's some hastily-thrown-together slop, on top of unhealthy wake-me-ups or empty calories just to fill your stomach.
Hardly what I'd call a good attitude to health! There's plenty of people worried about you!
Doctor, if you want to get strong, you need a good work-life balance, to eat three square meals a day, and to get some exercise! Got that?
Thanks for helping us out, Doctor. The same goes to everyone at Rhodes Island. I've made a lot of new friends here.
If only Falin were here too...
After we're done eating, could you give us a detailed overview of this land?
What? Why're you staring at me, Doctor? What, you want a drink? Well, it's not that you can't, it's just... Are you an adult yet?
I really can't tell with that mask on...
Oh, this is delicious!
I need to note down the recipe. We've got to make it for Falin once we save her!
I'll have to tell her all about the journey we went on to save her, about all the lovely people we met along the way, and all the memories we made...
I'm sure she'll love hearing about it!
